choices: Recruiting and managing her quirky staff.
Checking out some hot new properties to expand her brand.
Her rival Kitchen Bosses have been getting up in her business.
Gloria has a bonafide crisis on her hands to remedy stat!
She's still peckish and has several new recipes to try.
1.content:
Gloria Vandergast loved her office.

It was the ultimate status symbol for anyone who was their own boss. Rich wood paneling, an artfully sturdy mahogany desk, framed accolades on the wall next to her degrees and a city view out the window. And her ergonomic out-sized office chair that had to be custom made.

All her life Gloria had never been skinny. Who would trust a skinny chef, anyways? You could tell from her size that she loved food just as much as she loved ruling over the kitchen with an iron fist. Currently she tipped the scales at around the two hundred pound mark, which filled out her five foot four inch frame considerably. She had a thick middle, rounded hips, pudgy thighs and a moderately sized chest for someone of her frame. Her skin was dark tan and her hair a darker midnight black, kept shoulder length whenever she didn’t tie up her hair for the kitchen.

She favored clothes that made a statement and her typical business suit was no exception. A maroon, velvet accented suit with pencil skirt ending just above the knee. Like her chair it also had to be custom made for her size, as the original style of the suit was for the petite body. Gloria liked it for being just snug enough, a bold statement piece that made her chest look more impressive when combined with her favorite bra. Although a few of the buttons were starting to look strained…

Gloria was a shrewd and driven businesswoman. She had graduated with masters for both culinary arts and business degrees, a skilled chef who didn’t want to labor under anyone else’s kitchen. So she started her own company, becoming a Kitchen Boss looking to build her brand with a backbone of fantastic kitchens and any other business to cater to her customers' lifestyle.

She knew her food could be fattening and her restaurants were criticized for targeting the overweight and obese. Gloria didn’t care; in her eyes the fat demographic would bring her the most wealth. Why not have have “whales” for her standout patrons, or whales? She'd feed them for breakfast, lunch, or dinner if it meant her wallet expanded faster than their waistline. It was a growing customer base in more ways than one.

Gloria’s dominate business strategies followed along the lines of her vices: Envy, Greed, and Gluttony. And not always in that specific order. Gloria couldn’t stand anybody having something she didn’t have, be it property or acclaim, especially if it was one of her rival Kitchen Bosses. Gloria always wanted more, she wanted to make big money and was never fully satisfied with the status quo. And she was a big eater, constantly snacking and tasting as she perfected her recipes, savoring her food far too messily in contrast to the image of a successful Kitchen Boss that she wanted to present. Each of her vices naturally fed into the next, part of what had made her the boss she was today…although left unchecked that could be disastrous.

“You give me half price on those shoddy, already rotting fruits you call ‘organic produce’ or I’m taking my business elsewhere!” Gloria bullied a supplier on the other end of the phone over her morning latte and pastry. “Or you’ll never sell greens in this town again. I’ve got a lot of clout with the local chamber of commerce.”

She hung up the phone smugly as she took a big bite of her apple-cream cheese danish, knowing her supplier would have to meet her price or be finished. Closing a deal always got Gloria fired up for the day. She couldn't spend all day sitting back over fancy coffee and her pastry. She had a budding kitchen empire to run, after all.

What was the first order of the day?
1.title: The Life of a Kitchen Boss

choices: Erika
Tsukiko
Emma
Sasha
Garnet
11.content:
Today was most likely one of the greatest days in Gloria Vanderghast's life.

Not only was she opening a brand new kitchen known as "Gloria's Steakhouse" within the big city of Trussel within a week, her first restaurant within a widely populated city, but she had also received hundreds of job offers from chefs all across the state to become chefs at her new kitchen. She had already hired another chef to work under her, a tan-skinned rotund woman by the name of Nicole Harker, but now she was looking over dozens of other chefs, so she could choose which one to pick.

Driving her way to Gloria's Steakhouse, the Kitchen Boss would quickly notice said chef walk up as well. "Ah, Nicole. You up here to check out the meals of the other chefs too?"

"Yeah, can't miss out a good meal after all. Who knows, we may even find someone who actually would work well here..."

Standing at a minuscule 4'10", Nicole would end up being even shorter than Gloria, which was somewhat impressive considering the Kitchen Boss's own short stature. However, despite being shorter, Nicole would appear to be the heavier one, weighing in at around 50 more pounds than her boss and appearing even fatter due to the weight being localized in a smaller area. Not only was she looking somewhat obese, but her hips in particular were two huge pillars of fat, which the woman took pride in whenever it would shake.

"Ha, yeah I can't wait to enjoy these meals as well... who knows, maybe we'll find something that's actually good, and then we can have another great chef like you!"

Walking into the soon to be open steakhouse, the two chefs would end up seeing a wide assortment of women of all sizes and weights, all trying to bring their own tasty meals into the hiring contest. Taking their time, the two would eat through every meal, offering their thoughts on each meal.

"Bleh! This steak is all raw! Next!"

"Hmm... this is pretty good... for a dive bar! Next!"

"Hmm... this is actually really tasty, but not what I'm looking for since this is supposed to be a steakhouse, not a bakery. I'll call you as a first hire for if I do open a dessert place, but for now NEXT!"

After a long while of tasting and stuffing themselves with multiple dishes, the two would eventually narrow down the potential chefs down to five women, all of them having a high level of skill. The first one would be a woman named Erika, who while surprisingly skinny had somehow perfected the art of cooking meat, able to give it a amazing taste simply from roasting it and giving it the most succulent of spices.

Next up would be a rather hip-heavy Asian woman named Tsukiko, weighing about the same as Gloria but quite a bit taller at 6'0", making her appear merely chubby rather than being full-on fat. She had brought in a succulent batch of fries, proving her skills in using a fryer properly, making it taste as if it were never fried, but still carrying the crunchy exterior.

Third would be a huge, 6'5" woman named Emma, who was rather top heavy while carrying a rather prominent gut, looking like she would weigh more than Gloria if the two were at the same height. She admittedly had the least pleasant tastes out of the 5, but her plating skills made the food look very appetizing, which could make her be good for plating duties and serving rather than specifically just cooking.

After Emma would be another skinny chef, by the name of Sasha. With Sasha though, she had brought the fifth and final chef, a rather obese chef woman by the name of Garnet with her, and the two seemed to work together as a pair of chefs. This proposition seemed to work very well, as Garnet was used to taste testing and making sure the food tasted right and Sasha could much more easily cook with her skinny frame. Together, the two were able to make possibly the best meal that the group had tried during the entire day.

After the last batch of dishes though, the two women would lie down in their chairs, rubbing their full guts as Gloria would look to Nicole. "Man, most of those meals were pretty garbage, but some of those people made amazing grub. That duo, Sasha and Garnet for example..."

"Don't forget *bwoarp* Emma..."

"Oh yeah, and definitely Erika too... and then there's Tsukiko... ah those fries were heavenly..."

Discussing over the potential chefs, the two would eventually decide to call back the five women tomorrow, one at a time to discuss their deals and positions within the steakhouse. Going home for the rest of the day after looking after her other kitchens, Gloria would lie down and enjoy a bit of pizza (self made, of course), before going to rest for the night, anticipated for tomorrow.

The next morning, Gloria would call...
11.title: Recruiting and managing her quirky staff.

choices: With all these new hires, Gloria's Steakhouse opens to great acclaim!
Opening night underperforms, there's loads of leftover steaks.
Tsukiko is an excellent chef but has some quirks Gloria didn't anticipate.
Some of the other hires feel snubbed at being passed over for Tsukiko.
Gloria gathers the remaining four tryouts only to fire them, creating rivals.
112.content:
Gloria's first order of business the following day was calling Tsukiko back to her steakhouse. Being the first call of five, Tsukiko was scheduled for a late morning interview. Nicole was mildly surprised of the arrangement when Gloria told her over cappuccino.

"Really?" Nicole chuckled. "Just from her fries? Although those were killer."

"I was going over the footage from yesterday while prepping myself a pizza," Gloria began, queuing up a clip on her tablet. She and Nicole had spent the cooking audition mainly judging by results plate by plate. The new steakhouse had numerous security cameras in the house and back, ostensibly for security purposes. However, they also provided a valuable look into the applicants' preparation of their meals.

Gloria had built a large kitchen in the steakhouse, large enough that even dozens of corpulent chefs could work on their dishes during the waves of demonstrations. Being one of the taller heads of the new recruits, the six foot Tsukiko could be easily seen with the blue streak of hair she had in her long, tied back black locks. The kitchen was only "just" large enough, as there were bumps and collisions from the various cooks all competing for space and ingredients. This volume of potential talent trying to prove their best steaks might have soured some applicants' chances from all the stepping on toes and bumping of booties, yet Gloria both wanted the process done quickly and also wanted to see how well her new chefs could handle a challenge.

"Whoa!" Nicole gasped, seeing the footage that had impressed Gloria. One of the failed tryouts and Emma, another tall cook, had ran into each other next to the spice rack as one reached for a potato peeler and Emma sought the cayenne pepper. The big and bulky Emma stood firm, causing the smaller tryout to bounce back and create a chain reaction of chefs finding their cooking interrupted. Tsukiko, on the wrong end of this accident, was just getting her fries out of the grease as a wayward collision struck her. The basket was screwed out of her hand, in all honesty the fries should have been sent to the floor.

With quick reflexes, Tsukiko caught her balance and swiftly brought the fry basket back into position, catching the batch of fries without letting one drop.

"Oooh! Ooooh!" Nicole would often bounce in her seat or gesticulate wildly when overjoyed, her mighty hips quaking as she reacted to the video of Tuskiko's performance. "That save! Her knife work! That dodge!"

Indeed, although chubby on her tall frame, Tsukiko had smooth dexterity and footwork when it came to avoid other obstacles in the kitchen that tied up other chefs in a frenzy to plate up impressive steak dinners. Rapidly chopping garlic and chives, deftly sidestepping another tryout as she took her sauce off the stove, and keeping her plate perfectly poised as the swinging door to the kitchen swung towards her violently as a crying applicant was axed, Tsukiko clearly had the moves.

"She was in the top three for steaks," Gloria continued her judgement. "And while I want the best who know how to handle their meat, a steak dinner isn't a 'dinner' until you've got a good side with it. And her fries were the best, hands down."

"So Tuskiko comes out on top?" Nicole asked.

"I think she has promise, yes. She's shown to be well rounded in the kitchen and knows how to avoid a spill. And I imagine this place to be hopping once we open up, I want those steaks coming out good and fast." Gloria drew up a simple hierarchy chart. "If you're my head chef, Nicole, I want Tsukiko as your sous-chef. Everybody else answers to you two."

Gloria paused to hold in a belch. "Of course, when I'm in the kitchen I'm the number one chef, got it?"

"Yes ma'am!" Nicole mock saluted.

"Ms. Vandergast?" speaking of their star candidate, Tsukiko arrived. Poised, the moon faced applicant stood at attention in her grey chef's smock, a messy folded apron and scruffy boots; a sign of her hard work ethic, a contrast to her prim posture much like her blue streak of hair.

"Tsukiko, honey, you may call me Gloria," the franchise owner preened, although a bit miffed to be considered a 'Ms.' She was not past her prime, hell no! "But it's boss when we're in the kitchen together, no question."

"Yes, boss." Tsukiko bowed politely.

"Those fries of yours were absolutely kicker, Tsukie," Nicole said, already finding a nickname for her new sous-chef. "How'd you know your way around the fryer so well?"

"I worked ten summers on my uncle's takoyaki truck when I was a teen," Tsukiko replied simply. "We had a lot of fried foods on the menu."

"She's like some sort of fry-up samurai!" Nicole giggled before Gloria shushed her.

"Well welcome to the team, Tsukiko," Gloria said. "We're going to be frying up loads of great stuff with our steaks and you're going to be number two. Except when I reign in the kitchen."

"Thank you very much!" Tuskiko bowed again.

"I've got one change in mind on your fries before they get on the menu," Gloria continued, almost puffing out of her maroon suit with burgeoning authority. "And how about you demonstrate some takoyaki and calamari. Say, four or five dozen per batch." A hint of drool escaped her lips as she thought about possible changes to the menu. "Chop chop, ladies, we've got business to attend to!"
112.title: Tsukiko - Fryup Samurai

choices: They look great and happy about their work.
They look tired from serving so many customers...
They look starved, maybe they can make one more meal...
They look stressed, lacking respect for the chain of authority...
1121.content:
After Tsukiko being hired, over the course of the next few days Gloria would work through calling and hiring the other four women she found to be the best cooks, before searching out a few other of the mediocre chefs to fill out the remaining staff. While they wouldn't be as talented chefs compared to what she and Nicole were calling the "Main Five", they, in her own opinions, would be able to listen to their orders and at least work to the best of their abilities.

Eventually, opening night would come, and as she would try to fit herself into a fancy dress, she would make a video call to Nicole, who was already at the steakhouse preparing to open.

"Hey Nicole..." As she started the call, Gloria would finish getting the dress fitting, though it would look somewhat tight.

"Ah Gloria! Your looking a lot more fancy then usual... are you going on a night out on the town now that our Steakhouse is opening?"

"No, no... I'm actually heading out to the new kitchen in a bit. Don't worry, your still in charge, I'm just showing up as boss to watch business up close. I expect things to be ship-shape this first night though, gotta make sure that we bring up a amazing work effort due to our kitchen being open after all..."

"Ah, of course!" Nicole would bow a bit for the Kitchen Boss from her end, before looking to the kitchen. "So, would you like us to make you something for when you arrive?"

"Hmm... three steaks should do, with a side of fries with each steak. As for the drink... surprise me."

"Understood. I'll tell Erika and Tsukiko to focus on your meal first and foremost when they get here, and I'll take to work delivering it to your table when you get here..."

"Good. I'll see you in thirty minutes then. Bye!"

Hanging up, Gloria would head out on her way to Trussels from her hotel room, riding a taxi to "Gloria's Steakhouse" to keep herself from being noticed until she was at the very least at the kitchen itself. Getting out of the taxi, she would very quickly notice that the kitchen itself was packed almost to it's limits, leaving the boss very happy.

Walking in, she would quickly find herself a booth table, and as she sat down she would wait for Nicole to bring her the food she had ordered. Once the meal was placed down, she would take the glass and sip it down, not even looking at the glass before determining the drink.

"Hmm... Cabernet Red... perfect. Thank you so much Nicole, expect a pay raise after this day's over..."

"R-really? Thank you so much!"

Watching the woman walk her way back to the kitchen, her hips shaking wildly with each step, Gloria would smile a bit as she turned back to the meal she had ordered. Taking her time with each part of her big meal, she would feel a heavenly taste from the superbly cooked steak, as well as an amazing crispiness from the steak fries.

It would not just be from Gloria that good experiences would come from though, as the Kitchen Boss would hear from the crowds and tables...

"Mmm, this takoyaki is amazing! Hey, can I get another order?"

"Compliments to the chef, I've never tasted such amazing steak and fries before!"

"Ah, who would have thought steak could have been this good... we gotta come back here again this week hubby..."

Eventually though, after Gloria had finished her meal, she would stay around, watching the crowd of customers up until closing. Listening to their various praises and compliments, the head chef would feel especially prideful and proud, as she had never heard such praise in any of her previously opened up restaurants. By the time the last of the customers would walk out, she would eventually walk to the back of the store, to view her now-prized chefs...
1121.title: Opening Night!

choices: Feeling insulted by Erika, Gloria takes the reigns in the kitchen next night.
Gloria caves in to give herself, Nicole and the Main Five a mental health day.
It's a managerial headache, but Gloria tries to implement some staff suggestions
The Boss's word is the law, as Gloria keeps rule with an iron hand.
11212.content:
"Excellent performance tonight, everyone," Gloria said as she entered the kitchen, quite proud that her design and management of her new restaurant had gone off without a hitch. "I think we've got an immediate clientele here so if we keep it...up..."

Her enthusiasm appeared to be the only enthusiasm in the room. Her staff had no energy, as they had given their all to the opening night service. Now, Gloria saw that everyone was teetering around the kitchen, finding awkward places to rest, moaning like the waking dead. Walking would be too much effort.

Nicole was barely visible. The short, thigh-expansive cook was sitting on the floor panting as she fanned herself, looking quite unprofessional compared to her role of Gloria's head chef at the newly christened steakhouse. Tsukiko, the sous chef, was leaning against an open section of tiled wall, the most nimble in the kitchen also past her limits as she had a sweat rag over her forehead as she took long sips from a cola bottle.

Emma, the tallest cook given she had five inches on Tsukiko, was slumped over the prep table with dingy blonde hair falling over her face where it was tied when she had been tasked to plating and sauces. Her large, muscular yet flabby bulk filled the table between spent dishes and cookware. The skinniest chefs of the bunch, Erika and Sasha, had ended up back to back barely able to support the other upright. The red headed, highland accented Erika had assisted Tsukiko with sides, with the dark brown haired, willowy Sasha on desserts and other entrees. Rounding out the "Main Five" under Nicole was Sasha's partner, the incredibly obese Garnet seated up at the pass for final inspection of plates leaving the kitchen. The African-American Garnet had culinary knowledge and a sophisticated palette on par Gloria's high standards on food, given she was the brains behind her and Sasha's top tier steak dinner application. However, speed was needed in the kitchen, more than what Garnet could provide, so they had found three stools to support her enormity as she ensured quality left the kitchen. And yet she looked as sweaty as the other, more active chefs.

Besides the line cooks, other kitchen assistants, busboys, and waitstaff had also sought out whatever free chair or open space there was to slump exhausted. No closing procedures were underway as everyone lazed about the kitchen left messy from opening night.

"What gives?" Gloria demanded, putting hands on her plump hips, feeling slight tension after enjoying her three steaks that night. "We've got cleanup, closing, lockup. I want to be out of here before midnight!"

"Yeah...boss..." Nicole panted from the floor. "I know we kicked ass out there...with great dinners...but I need ten minutes..."

"Really?" Gloria prodded her contemporary with her shoe. "This is the example you want to lead with? We all know it's not over until the house is shut up. There's a followup health inspection this week."

"Ma'am," Tsukiko offered patiently, "That was the biggest opening night I've ever seen. Many of us had seen. Please give us some time to recover."

"Some time to recover?" Gloria was incredulous. "No, you strike when the iron is hot!" She clapped her hands for emphasis. "We can't rest on our laurels after opening night. We need to think bigger! I want this place to be the hottest steakhouse in the city. The crowd tonight is a paltry start in comparison."

"Paltry start?" Erika spoke up. "Ms Vandergast, I've worked some tough kitchen be'fore, believe me, but you cannae work us to the bone each night if this is your expect'd default. E'van with our collected talent we'll burn out!"

"Look at this talk," Gloria said, disgusted. "I thought I was hiring the best of the best when it comes to steaks. You all sound ready to quit!"

"Not quit," Emma said, rising up to her full height. "We just need a quieter pace. Opening is an outlier, right? We won't always be this busy on an average day."

Although Emma had the size to dwarf Gloria, the ambitious Kitchen Boss got right up in her face. "Average day? That mindset doesn't led to continued excellence. There should always be room for improvement. What do you all think would improve this situation?"

Garnet raised her hand from where she was precariously perched by the pass, having found some steak tips to munch on. "Less nights we're open a week?" she offered.

"More general help," Sasha added, "so we can rotate our shifts."

Others on staff spoke up with ideas. "Maybe an employee lounge on site where we can take our own meals?"

"What about a digital ordering system at each table to cut down on traffic?"

"Is it too early to ask for a raise?"

"ENOUGH!" Gloria roared. "Whose name is on the establishment again? Mine. 'Gloria's Steakhouse,' and I didn't get to this point in my career by taking it easy! By playing safe! If you've attracted the kinds of crowds we did on opening you can't lower the bar from there or you'll lose customers. No, you push it higher. Do any of you have your own restaurants? No, but I know what it takes to succeed."

"Yeah, and what all did yae do tonight?" Erika scoffed.

"Easy, easy girls," Nicole finally pulled herself upright, getting as close as she could to Gloria's eye level as the head boss looked ready to commit murder. "Tensions are just run-in' high after tonight, that's all." Breathing heavily, she found a scrap of remaining fries to chew as she gestured with the other fry. "We know we can work as a team if we're all in this together, right? Nobody's saying this was a bad night."

"But," she gave her partner a pleading look, "Gloria, honey, you've got to cut us a little slack so we can dig in our roots. We've got a champion team in the making, I know it." She leaned in closer to whisper. "But if you break them too hard, too soon, I'm not saying there's gonna be a mutiny, but things might turn ugly."

Getting her breathing under control, Gloria restrained some of her spitfire vitriol. "Alright, fine! Take what time you need to catch your breath. But this place better be spic 'n' span by the time we open tomorrow, you've got that?"

There were various murmurs of reply. If morale was going to be an issue at her new steakhouse, Gloria needed to find a solution fast.
11212.title: Deflation of Success

choices: 101 Harbor Road
62 Newtrail Way
555 Main Street
Paradigm Plaza
Malanor Manor
12.content:
"Ah, Ms. Vandergast?"

Gloria's gaze swiveled to the door, where she could see her blonde-haired assistant nervously poking her head in. She hid a grimace at the younger woman's apprehension. No, that wouldn't do at all. If Danni really wanted to make a place for herself in Gloria's burgeoning empire, she would need to straighten up; literally, in this case. "Spine straight!" the chubby chef barked. Danni flinched at first, but quickly obeyed the order and stood up straight so fast that her chest wobbled for a bit. She still looked way too nervous, but at least she wasn't slouching now. Nodding her approval, Gloria gestured Danni to come in.

The buxom blonde was still a little apprehensive, but she spoke with a little more force than the nervous warble Gloria usually attributed to her. Good, she might just make it yet. "I brought the files on the properties you were looking into, Ma'am. I also received a call from Ms. Harker. She said traffic is a lot heavier than she expected, and estimated that she'd be at least an hour late, maybe longer."

Gloria frowned at the second bit of news, which caused Danni to flinch again. Seeing this caused Gloria to sigh; it was no use taking out her frustration on the messenger, she supposed. "Print a second set of files for Nicole to have when she gets here. When you're done with that, start prepping the paperwork for our offers once I make a decision."

"Of course, Ma'am. If you'll excuse me?" With a wave of her hand, Gloria dismissed the younger woman, who left the office as quickly as she could without seeming rude. Gloria, meanwhile, began to peruse the files she'd been given. This was one of her favorite parts of the business; expansion. Every new restaurant up and running was another province in her proverbial empire, and Gloria's ambitions were not yet sated. Sadly, there was only enough money in her coffers to afford to build one new restaurant this time, so she'd need to be careful.

The first location she looked at was at 101 Harbor Road. A scenic spot overlooking the nearby marina, a restaurant built there would have fantastic views of the ocean during the evening, a surefire way to draw in couples looking for a romantic evening. It would also provide a good incentive to renegotiate some contracts Gloria had with her seafood providers; since the property was right by the ocean, it would mean smaller transportation costs to get said seafood from the fishing boats over to the kitchen.

Second in the packet was 62 Newtrail Way. The property was built at the base of a mountain that brought in hikers from around the country, and Gloria was certain that they'd enjoy having a spot to sit down and enjoy a good meal after hours of walking and thin air. At least she assumed so, since Gloria had never been one for hiking herself. The restaurant's view of the mountain might also bring in those who weren't hikers but enjoyed natural scenery to go along with their dinners (that, Gloria did understand).

Next up was 555 Main Street. Instead of the views of nature, this restaurant brought with it command of the city center. Main Street ran from City Hall through the business and shopping districts on into the suburbs, with plentiful highway access throughout. Building a restaurant here might very well attract the attention of the politicians and businessmen who liked to spend big on their meals to impress the various clients, cronies, and yes-men who accompanied them. If someone was going to be receiving those big payouts, why shouldn't it be Gloria Vandergast? She was certain she could actually give them their money's worth when it came to the food.

After that was a spot at Paradigm Plaza. The Plaza was one of the biggest shopping malls in the entire city, and a magnet for youths looking for places to shop, hang out, and generally not be around their parents for as long as possible. If Gloria could hook the next generation and make one of her restaurants the place where they came when they were hungry, she could have a group of customers that kept on coming back for years and years. The tricky part would be finding the proper marketing tactic to appeal to "the youths of today" (Gloria grimaced again. Was she getting old? Surely not!), but she figured she'd figure something out if she needed to.

The last property she wanted to look at was the most unusual by far, being the old Malanor Manor. As the story went, the house had been built by Maximilian Malanor, a businessman who had made his fortune in the railroad boom of the mid-1800s. Then one night, something had gone horribly wrong, and Maximilian and his wife Marianne, both childless, had disappeared. Rumors floated around about how the manor was haunted. Fueling those rumors was the fact that every business that had acquired the property went bankrupt not long after the sale was finalized. Gloria was not a superstitious woman, though. She made her own destiny! And having a restaurant built in a supposedly-haunted house would definitely provide an experience that none of her rivals could come close to offering.

The hardest part about this whole process was the fact that she could only purchase one property at the moment. A part of Gloria was tempted to go into debt to try and snap them all up at once. But she was a student of history, and the Kitchen Boss knew that growing her empire too quickly was a recipe for disaster. No, she'd just stick to the one new property for now.

Eventually Nicole Harker did arrive, still rather miffed about the traffic jam that had delayed her. Her frustration was soon forgotten as the two weighty executives found themselves in a whirlwind discussion about the merits and potential pitfalls of each of the locations. It took the entire morning for them to make a decision, but as they broke for lunch, Gloria knew where the next restaurant in her empire was going to be located.
12.title: Location, Location, Location!

choices: Tropical surf & fun, Gloria settles on a beach theme.
Scuttle those bilge rats! Gloria settles on a pirate theme.
Renovations begin on staff features, but new complications arise.
This is a bad time for one of Gloria's staff to find they have a seafood allergy
121.content:
“You're building a restaurant near the coast? Ooh, maybe we can theme it around the beach, have chefs wearing something a bit revealing! That’ll certainly draw in crowds, right Gloria?”

Standing in the now bought property on 100 Harbor Road, Gloria and Nicole would both be observing the new locale that they now owned. They were testing every chair, trying every booth seat, and checking for any weak floorboard, in order to make sure the place they bought was actually safe.

Thankfully, once they were done checking, aside from a squeaky board near the entrance, the place seemed to be fine. A few booth seats would need to be more spaced to allow bigger residents to sit in them, but for now the soon to be restaurant looked ready to serve.

The only thing that they had to decide on now was a theme though, and that would be a challenge.

“So, what do you think Gloria? Can we please do the beach theme? I’d finally have the chance to try out this new bikini I got last year, and I won’t have to wear such a restricting chef uniform...”

Looking around, Gloria would sigh a bit. “I admit, that is somewhat of a interesting idea, but I was more thinking of a pirate theme. Would certainly help sell food with how popular they seem to be in recent years.”

“Maybe... but at least consider! Besides, this building’s partially on the harbor, maybe if we don’t do that theme we could at least have a small place in the break room to relax and swim after hours?”

“Hmm... I’ll definitely do that, could use a good break after every workday myself. But as for the theme... I’ll probably sleep over it, and call you back in the morning on what theme we should go with. You think that’s fair?”

Checking the back of the kitchen as she was asked this, Nicole would sigh a bit, as she would feel the water flowing underneath the back of the store. “I guess that’s fair at least. So you wanna cook some early dinner with me while we finish checking this place out?”

Feeling her stomach growl, Gloria would only nod as the two would end up working together, cooking up with some supplies they had already brought over, cooking up some slices of salmon and shrimp into a meal fit for four. Smelling the completed meal, the two would set it down on the nearest booth before digging into it together.

After a while, eventually the two would finish off this seafood platter, their stomachs stretched to their limits as they would lie down in their booth seats. Rubbing her own belly, Gloria felt happy for the first time in quite a bit, probably due to trying out some of her own food, and Nicole would seem to be in a similar state.

“One thing’s for certain at least Nicole... the menu is definitely going to involve a bunch of seafood on top of the burgers we serve at every one of our locations. You feel okay about that at least?”

“Oh yeah, totally. Man, I haven’t had popcorn shrimp in such a while but it tastes so much better than I imagine. We should eat together more often...”

“Agreed.” Helping Nicole squeeze out of her booth seat after squeaking out of it herself, Gloria would help carry her fellow chef with her out of their new location, with the intent of turning it into her next restaurant. She already had a crew in mind for this place, now all she needed was a theme...
121.title: Gloria’s Beachfront Property

choices: The three decide to continue their night together elsewhere...
The next morning, Gloria decides to invite her sister over to try her new place.
The next morning, Gloria receives a call about someone sneaking into the Barrel.
1213.content:
Over the course of the next few weeks, Gloria and Nicole would eventually settle for a balance between the two ideas, having a bit of a pirate theme but allowing the chefs to still wear some less restrictive pirate-themed swimwear as they cooked as part of the theme. With everything picked out though, all that was left was to finish up the break room and everything would be ready for a grand opening.

Working around the clock, Gloria would have some of the best workers in all of Trussel start preparing a swimming area in the personal quarters of the place. As the workers would begin cutting a hole into the floor to open up a swimming area however, the workers would text back at her, with something to say.

FELIX: Hey Glo, something is going on at the place we’re finishing our work on.

GLORIA: What? Surely your not trying to say the pool will not work, right?

FELIX: Well... it would be best if you came and saw it yourself. It’s... certainly unique.”

GLORIA: ...Fine. You’ve piqued my interests at least...

___

Showing up at the Pirate Barrel after a short drive, Gloria would look up at what a few weeks worth of construction has done to her store, before smiling. Only a few days from now this place will open, and once it does the revenue will be pouring in. If it was successful enough, she thought, it could even let her move to the nationwide market!

Reminding herself of what needed to be done though, Gloria would walk right in, being guided in by the construction workers into the back room, where she could see her office in all of it’s glory. Not only did it have a comfy seat and a great view of a TV, but with the pool being nearby it gave off the energy of her being rich and powerful, which she loved.

Looking at her trusted construction man Felix, Gloria would nod to him as she would look back to the pool. “So, what was it you were texting me about huh? Is there something troublesome with the pool?”

“Nah, it’s not about the pool itself. We can make it easy... it’s just... well, look inside of it. You’ll understand there.”

Looking down into the pool, Gloria would see a heavy amount of fish swimming around in the soon-to-be pool, making it somewhat hard to see the bottom of it. Thinking a bit about the fish, Gloria would feel through the water, and pull one of them, a small sea bass, out of the water.

“This is it? They’re just fish, they’re not going to bother anything. Maybe if there were sharp rocks I’d reconsider, but come on. You’ve dealt with worse...”

“I’m sorry, it’s just the principle of the thing.” Rubbing his construction hat, Felix would look down into the pool, as fish would continue to swim in a circular pattern down there. “We would probably get a lot of flack from activists if we disturbed them, and that would be bad for both of our businesses. Don’t you agree?”

Taking a bit of time to think about it, Gloria would sit down, before nodding a bit. “You got me there. I don’t want to exactly ruin my image just to make a pool. Hmm... you think you can keep the fish, but build it around them?”

Blinking a bit in confusion, Felix would tilt his head. “I’m sorry, what?”

“You heard me, building around them, while keeping them in! And then maybe having a door at the bottom to allow fish to freely swim in and out. That way, their environment won’t be disturbed, and we’ll still have a place to swim in!”

Sighing a bit, Felix would still look down in the pool with worry. “That would take quite a bit longer to finish building. At least a few days to ensure that it all works like that. But I will say, it should be possible to do that.”

“Good.” Quickly sliding a small raise to Felix, Gloria would leave as she would wait for the pool to be done, so that she could finally open. This day would come almost a full week later, and after a hard day of working at another of her own eateries, Gloria and Nicole would walk in to see their new Pirate Barrel minutes before closing.

As they would head off to the back of the kitchen, they would see two of the more subordinate chefs already packing up for leaving, with the third, more experienced chef already swapping out for a bikini now that cooking was done for the day.

This chef, by who Gloria and Nicole would recognize as Tsukiko Mihachi, was one of Gloria’s first picks when it came to running this seafood place, due to her expertise in frying. She had a rather plump figure with some rounded hips, and while her breasts were somewhat small compared to her two heavier co-workers, she managed to at least have somewhat of a small gut.

Walking up to her already in their own swimsuits, Gloria and Nicole would smile as they would see Tsukiko rock her red bikini. Noticing their approach, Tsukiko would wave to them a bit, before bowing. “Ah, it’s nice to see you two here. This first day was pretty exhilarating, and all the other chefs really enjoyed working here. I guess you were going to try out the pool?”

“Yep!” Grabbing the side of her swim suit, Nicole would stretch the rubber a bit before letting it go, causing her fat belly and breasts to jiggle. “This was my idea, and I’m going to be the first in it!”

“Relax, Nicole... we will get there, but for now, let’s have a bit of a snack prepared for while we swim.”

Taking a look around in the kitchen, Gloria would quickly, with the help of Tsukiko, cook up a large bucket full of popcorn shrimp, before dumping them all onto a second, water-resistant bucket that she had brought with her. Once it was done, she would beckon the other two to follow her as she would quickly dip into the pool.

As the night would go on, the three would relax and swim through the place, enjoying themselves as they marveled at the sight of various fish swimming beneath and beside them and having some popcorn shrimp to snack upon. Eventually though...
1213.title: Swimming with the Fishes

choices: To great surprise, Gloria does create an exciting business at Malanor Manor.
Her plans of renovation hit a snag with a series of unfortunate setbacks.
Gloria calls on her design team as they continue to explore the estate.
Although nothing paranormal occurs, there is something weird at the mansion.
125.content:
"You're really going to try to turn this creepy old estate around?" Nicole said after she and Gloria parked in front of the namesake mansion. Her top chef had not been in the camp of purchasing Malanor Manor given its history. Its reputation as a money sink to the last three businesses that had purchased and failed to turn a profit had kept anyone from buying the cheap listing. That didn't also take into account the urban legends surrounding the disappearance of the original owners and infamy in the local ghost hunting community.

"This is an unparalleled opportunity to drag a worthy venue up out of the depths," Gloria said, not dissuaded in her choice of property purchase. "So it has these phony ghost stories surrounding it. Pah! I consider that air of mystique to make it a killer haunt for the night scene."

"I really wish you hadn't used that choice of words," Nicole replied.

"I'm sensing a popular night outing," Gloria continued, passing over the aged cobblestone drive towards the veranda. "Perhaps an exclusive supper club. Vacationers want to visit a place with storied history, that's all the rage these days. Imagine what bank we could make off of that old legend!"

"Imagine what bank it'll take to get this up and running," Nicole said, not used to being the voice of reason compared to Gloria. She winced as the creaky, worn boards of the steps cried out as Gloria stepped up to the door.

Malanor Manor was a textbook definition of a fixer upper. The four-story mansion was built by Maximillian Malanor, an small time yet nevertheless rich industrialist during the 1800s railroad boom that helped put the city of Trussel on the map. Set in the outskirts of the county, the lavish estate had been an impressive residence of post-Victorian architecture back when it was constructed. Maximillian had built the house as a wedding gift to his wife Marianne, intended to be a home for their family. Yet the Malanors had never had children. After a grand house warming party three weeks after the building was finished, Maximillian & Marianne faded out of public eye to never be seen again. The mystery of their disappearance was lost to history. Some say the Malanors were murdered, others said they fled the country under false identities. Fringe conspiracies suggested it an act of witchcraft, an ancient curse, or even alien abductions that led to the house being abandoned.

Looting and squatting occurred in the centuries since. Adventure capitalists of the past had tried to purchase the estate for either residential or commercial use, yet nobody lived there for long and previous businesses had failed to take hold. The last three ventures from companies that had acquired Malanor Manor; a failed bed & breakfast, museum, and headquarters of an organic plantation brought the manor back on the market for Gloria to make a steal of a purchase.

"Well come on, Nicole!" Gloria put her hands on her hips in a huff. "I didn't hire you to hang back when a new opportunity becomes available to us!"

Nicole wasn't afraid of the supposed hauntings about the Manor but she had a real distrust of the dilapidated porch given she had a good fifty or so pounds on her boss. Steeling her nerves, she took tepid steps towards the door willing her fat legs to move.

Creak!

Creak!

Creak, CREAK, CRUNCH!

"Pleasebeonsolidground, please-be-on-solid-ground," Nicole cried as she barely leapt over the threshold, feeling that the last section of the veranda was going to give out from under her. Trying to regain her composure, the flabby head chef coughed from the dust riled up by hers and Gloria's entry. The tarnished foyer and grand staircase to the second level had seen better days. The smell of rot and stale air permitted the manor. With a parlor and greenhouse out of the side doorways from the foyer, abandoned materials from the last failed businesses could be seen in those rooms. Bagged furniture, the covers torn to shreds, and the derelict remains of old computer equipment still remained, modern touches that had failed to take off.

"Think of the view you could have from the upper floor," Gloria said.

"I am NOT climbing any more stairs today," Nicole would physically put her foot down for emphasis but she was still fearful of falling through the floor.

"What's got you so squirrelly, Nicole?" Gloria asked, her overflowing bravado blind to the drawbacks that could come from refurbishing a dilapidated manor, let alone the rumors of it being haunted.

"I'm just saying this whole venture sounds questionable. Nobody's been ever to turn over Malanor Manor for a profit."

"That's because the world hasn't seen what Gloria Vandergast is going to do with this property!" Gloria quipped, having that mad spark of an entrepreneurial innovation that had gotten her to this point in her career. "You'll be singing another tune, Nicole, once you see the space for the kitchen. The photos of the available space made it very intriguing to purchase."

"You do remember I'm pitch deaf, right?" Nicole sourly retorted. She got her kicks from her cringeworthy attempts at karaoke nights that would turn any audience the wrong way. But Nicole had no song in her heart for this crazed venture, hoping that the kitchen was on ground level. She drew the line at crossing any other sketchy floorboards.
125.title: Malanor Manor

choices: True to Nicole's concerns, Gloria & co are too weighty in the outdated mansion.
Gloria acts like a woman possessed, going over the top to remodel the Manor.
Sandra hopes to impress Gloria, discovering creepy secrets during her repairs.
Chalchi has outlandish ideas for decor, but her new food additives show promise.
1253.content:
"Great! Now my car's stalled," Nicole cried as she and Gloria were stranded in front of the old manor. The new owner of the estate had far more optimism about the lucrative restaurant potential of the kooky old house after a quick tour of the ground level. Nicole was more than relieved to have solid ground under her feet, as in the manor's current state it couldn't handle too many weighty individuals at once. Her relief was soon dashed with her continued car trouble.

"I'm sure it's due to idling in that traffic all morning," Gloria said, busy tapping at her phone. "You should get your car replaced if it's giving you trouble."

"I was kind of waiting for a big bonus," Nicole snarked, today going down into a history of her worst days.

"There will be no bonus if we don't get the new business in the black," Gloria shot back. "Never fear! I was reaching out to my design consultants and Sandra can bring out her family's tow truck."

"Consultants?" Nicole noticed the plural. "Oh no, you don't mean her as well." She trusted Gloria's on call handy-woman but the next number Gloria added to her group text brought the thigh heavy chef pause.

"Unlike you're doubts, Nicole, Chalchi has the eye to appreciate what the manor has to offer," Gloria said. "They say they can be here in fifty minutes."

"That's far too long!" Nicole whined. "I'll be hungry by then!"

"I've told them they need to make it in 30 minutes and they better bring us lunch." Ignoring that an extended brunch/lunch meeting was had by the lead Kitchen Boss and her number two chef when they were deciding which property to purchase.


Two vehicles made their way towards Malanor Manor, keeping in contact with short-band radio.

"Jade Skirted River to Grease Monkey Momma, come in. Do you copy, Grease Monkey Momma?"

The driver of the tow-truck from the 'Hwyler Family Automative & Towing' company radioed back. "Are the call signs really necessary, Chalchi?"

"I'm not going to ask for your bandwave and NOT use it, Grease Monkey Momma. Over." The driver of the 'Offbeat Painting & Salvage' van chuckled over the radio. "Bigger Fish and Hipped-Opatamus sure are the harsh taskmistresses, aren't they?"

"Who is who, again?" the tow truck driver asked. "Honestly, Chalchi, I'm just trying to not put a toe out of line. I'm still hoping to get Mz. Vandergast to sponsor my race car."

"Ten-forty and double-o-fifty, Grease Monkey Momma. We'll rendezvous at the horror house and feed the beast bosses with the takeout I picked up."

"None of that jargon means what you think it means," came the exasperated sigh. "Wait, you got them takeout? I thought I was supposed to?!"


"About time," Gloria noted from Nicole's car as the brown with red accents tow-truck and rainbow mural van made their way up the manor drive. Her two consultants emerged from their vehicles, bags of carryout in tow.

Sandra Hwyler was the younger of the pair, a promising if somewhat meek mechanic Gloria kept on her docket whenever heavy repairs were needed. Sandra had her fair brown hair in a bob cut. Her greasy overalls did little to hide her overdeveloped breasts and hips. Yet this was Sandra at her skinniest, maturing out of a childhood and teenage years being greatly overweight. Dedication to trying to improve her health, along with the eco-friendly technologies she fostered, kept her eyes for slimming down as she worked on her car in the local Alternative Fuel Racing League. A hometown girl wanting to improve the community, she wasn't sold on being on Gloria Vandergast's beck and call initially, yet her hopes of landing her as a big name sponsor in the racing circuit kept her loyal. Although her boss's tantalizing recipes were a further temptation.

Chalchi Kayode, in contrast, stood out the most from the heavy-set women assembled. Almost a decade Sandra's senior, Chalchi was tall and well built, her frame obscured by a thick, lurid-soiled painter's jumpsuit. She was also a wild spoken, overly imaginative Afro-Brazzillian house painter, artist, salvage operator and part time inventor. Foolhardy genius seemed to attract more of its own, as Gloria's continued patronage of Chalchi's wasn't only for interior painting. Chalchi dabbled in molecular gastronomy, such innovations in league with Gloria's crazed business acumen.

"Man, what a dump!" Chalchi said, taking off her goggles as she marveled at the old estate. White patches of missing pigment across her dark skinned face indicated her full body vitiligo otherwise covered by her work attire. "Let's chow down on the soul food quick, boss ladies, I've always wanted to check this place out. Officially."

"I, um, managed to fit in line at the Chik-fil-A," Sandra also offered some fast food, made lighter from her own nugget multipack eaten from some stress snacking. "The truck, I mean."

"The chow will be short, girls," Gloria said. "I want some design plans drafted up by this evening. We're going to make the most of Malanor Manor that it has to offer."

"And that it's made certifiably structurally sound," Nicole added, piggy digging into the proffered food.

Most of the food being gobbled up by Gloria and Nicole, the team made a second venture into the mansion despite Nicole's protests, akin to waiting several minutes to swim after eating on account of her continued suspicion of the manor supporting their collective weights. To placate these fears each of the women kept their distance as they continued to explore.

That's putting the engineering smarts to use, yet not the best idea when exploring a spooky manor. Horror films had less egregious prologues.

"You could really make a show out of this macabre architecture," Chalchi marveled, flashlight trained at the high archways on the second floor. "Like, spin up that haunted angle. Dinner theatre with a ghost sighting. Stay the night, if you dare!"

"Remember, we want repeat customers," Gloria announced from a room ahead. "Lots of tacky, outdated furniture. We can have nods to the past but I want classical antiques. It might require some subdued work form you, Chalchi."

"Subdued? Me??" Chalchi feigned disdain. "I'm betrayed, boss lady."

"I'm not turning this into one of your Carnivale-inspired-Halloweenie attractions," Gloria clarified, leaning back into the room oblivious to another house creak from her shifting weight. "Is there a mysterious allure to the most rumored to be haunted property in Trussel? Yes. But I'm still running a fine dining experience in the trappings of such secrets."

"You sure?" Testing the give of the guest bed, Chalchi stretched out on the dusty covers. "I've got a new formula for licorice paste. Great for cake decorating. Keeps its shape, so you can 3D print your own candy. Also an excellent adhesive."

"Focus, Chalchi!" Gloria grumbled. "And quit laying down on the job! Give me a price range for redoing the wallpaper and paint."

"Score!" Chalchi exclaimed, eager to cross another idiom off her 'Horrid Puns Bucket List' in a little notebook before she got to Gloria's request.


Meanwhile, Nicole Harker was perched at the top of the cellar steps, not about to find herself trapped in the basement with Sandra. Those were some jerky wooden stairs. "What's the damage, Sandy?" She called out. "Please tell me there's something to redeem this nightmare house."

"Well," Sandra summarized as her flashlight was trained over the utilities. "Boiler's out, that was to be expected. I think I see an area where we could feed in the gas lines." Looking down at the schematics, then back to the underpinnings of the manor, highlighted a puzzle for the wallflower mechanic. "Are you sure these are the original blueprints for the manor?"

"That's what Gloria got from the city offices," Nicole replied. "But they're not the originals, only surveyed results. They told us the original drafts were lost in a fire. More bad juju to surround this place, or whatever Chalchi would say," she spat.

"Then what was this switch box for?" Sandra asked, before pulling a lever.

A grinding, metallic sound buzzed in the wall behind Nicole. She yelped, about to jump out of her skin, if it wasn't for the pillars of fat in her legs, belly and backside weighing her down. "What the hell was that?!"

"I think," Sandra suggested, "that was something to do with the push-bell system. Or maybe some sort of inner-level dumbwaiter."

"What?" Nicole asked, confused.

"There's lots of strange, early electrical and mechanical systems in this house," Sandra said. "I'll need to inspect further. This is all analogue, nothing like the electrical systems of the day. It's a wonder any of the previous owners could turn on so much as a toaster."

"Great," Nicole sighed in resignation. "Just great. Why did Gloria decide to purchase a madhouse that's going to get us all bankrupt? Or killed, if we're unlucky."

"You really think the place is haunted?" Sandra called up, surprised.

"I'm not even sure this is a project she can turn around," Nicole said. "Not because of ghosts and spooks, but the whole building should be condemned. From my car breaking down to nearly falling through the porch, it's been ill omens since we got here."
1253.title: Bad Omens & Mixed Perspectives

choices: ...some kind of weird shrine. Huh?
...a library, filled with cookbooks.
...just a regular storeroom that they'd already looked in.
12531.content:
Eager to keep looking around, Gloria and Chalchi began trudging up to the third floor. Chalchi shined her flashlight at the wall, illuminating rectangular, darker spots on the wallpaper where pictures used to hang. "Boss lady, this place is gigantic! You really think you'll use all this space?" She looked back to find Gloria with her hands on her knees on the landing, catching her breath. "You good?"

She huffed. "I'm fine. I get... winded after I eat... fast food..." She looked up and flicked her hand at Chalchi. "Go on, I'm keeping up." She shrugged her shoulders and kept climbing the stairs to the hallway as Gloria kept speaking, a little behind. "And we'll be using all this space, for certain. I have big plans for this place. Huge plans. How's 'world's largest restaurant' sound to you?"

"That sounds amazing, boss."

"It'll be even better than you think." Gloria had caught up and was now walking backwards in front of Chalchi. "I want to make the guests FEEL like they're eating in a palace. The Versailles of restaurants, Vandergast sty- OOF!" Not looking where she was going, she backed straight into a locked door, hitting her head.

Chalchi giggled. "I was waiting for that to happen."

Gloria angrily pointed at her and shook her head wordlessly, then sighed and turned around. "What room do you think this is?" She jiggled the glass doorknob, confirming it was locked.

"You'll be remodeling this whole place, right?"

"Right. Nothing will be left untouched. This building will be restored to even further its former, glory, a testament-"

Chalchi cut her off. "Then there's one way to find out. Step aside." Gloria stepped aside as requested. Immediately after, Chalchi forcefully kicked the door square in its face, easily breaking it open. The wood splintered off its side where the lock was and swung open, hitting the inside wall with a loud bang. "There."

Gloria was confused. "You know we have the keys, right? All of them?"

She shrugged. "Some days you just want to kick a door." She walked inside, Gloria following close behind, floorboards creaking ominously as they entered. The now open room was pretty clearly the mater bedroom, or at least one of them anyway. It also seemed like it hadn't been touched in years, from even before the business ventures that had come in and out of the mansion. A grand bed sat in the center of the room, caked in dust. Light shone through a somewhat broken window, the beam illuminating dust particles that float in the air.

"Wow, Chalchi. Can you and Sandra remodel my bedroom to look like this?"

"Only if I can live there too, boss lady." Chalchi wiped her hand over a dirty mirror, revealing her reflection. She smiled at her image.

Gloria made her way over to the nightstand and picked up a framed photo, which she blew off. The photo was a portrait of a young woman who was quite beautiful, grinning big. "This must be Marianne Malanor. I wish I had her cheekbones."

Chalchi walked over to look. "Meh. Boring white lady." She put a hand to Gloria's mouth and gently squeezed her cheeks. "Nothing wrong with these chubby cheeks, boss."

Gloria grabbed her wrist and moved it away. "No touching. Unless I tell you to. Which I won't. I appreciate the compliment, though."

Chalchi grinned in response and quickly did another squeeze on her face, going, "Boop!" Then she walked away, as if it had never happened. Classic Chalchi, Gloria thought, why do I deal with this?

"Well," Gloria clapped her hands together and began to walk back towards the door, "we should get back downstairs. I need to show you the kitchen area. It's arguably the most impor- aaAAA" A massive crack underneath her cut her off. Before she knew it, the hardwood below her gave way and she was tumbling down through the floor, hitting the ground below with a thump.

"Holy fuck!" Chalchi rushed to the newly created hole and shone her flashlight down into it. It was hard to see much, as dust and wood particles were obscuring the beam. "Are you okay, boss lady?"

At the bottom of the hole, Gloria coughed. She shouted up, "I'm... I'm fine, I think. I'm heavy but I didn't think I was THAT heavy."

"The wood was probably rotten. You gotta be careful in places like this! Stay still, I'll go get the others. Here, catch!" She dropped her flashlight down the hole, landing next to Gloria. "See if you can find the door. Hang on!" Chalchi got up from her squat and briskly moved out of the room.

Dazed, Gloria picked up the flashlight and shone it around the room. She pieced together that she was in...
12531.title: Snap, crack, and plop

choices: Gloria decides to look into the Malanors more out of curiosity...
Complications arise as Sandra ends up suffering a injury while renovating.
In the dead of night, Gloria wakes up from a nightmare completely famished...
125311.content:
Turning the light on, Gloria would find quite a surprising look in the room she had found herself in. Unlike the rest of the rooms within the manor, the area she had found herself falling into was somehow in pristine condition. There were no working lights in the room, but the wooden walls seemed to be in perfect condition, at least on the side that she was on, and the wooden floor beneath looked somewhat fine.

As she would look around the room however, Gloria would find something really weird. The area seemed to be some large open ballroom, but in the center there would be some large statue, matching the looks of Marianne, but looking ten years older, and around as twice as heavy as Gloria herself was.

“What the...”

Kneeling down a bit, Gloria would eventually notice what would appear to be some sort of plaque attached to the bottom of the statue. Shining her light on the plaque, the chef would begin to slowly read it, to try and figure out what it said.

“Let’s see... ‘This Statue was made to honor Marianne Malanor, my wife. She was the light of my life, and although the house may be...’ Damn, the text is too faded off here...”

As she would try to get a closer look at the plaque, Gloria would quickly notice what would appear to be tiny gift boxes and bouquets of long-dead flowers surrounding the bottom of the statue, as well as some sort of giant circle painted into the ground. Picking up one of the gifts, Gloria would slowly open it, and see some sort of golden whisk inside.

“A golden whisk? What kind of weirdo leaves a golden- wait a minute... it all of these gifts are golden, I could be filthy rich by selling these bad boys!”

Grabbing each gift one by one, Gloria would quickly open them, revealing more and more golden cooking tools. Pocketing them at the moment, Gloria would toss the wrappings to the floor, as she would begin to move on from the statue.

As she would continue to use her flashlight to look around, Gloria would eventually find what would appear to be a door. Unlike the rest of the room, the door appeared to be much like the rest, rotting a bit but still working. Turning the knob, Gloria would quickly move out from the room and into the hallway, slowly looking around as she would try to reunite with Chalchi.

Thankfully, it wouldn’t be long before Chalchi herself would end up finding Gloria, as the latter would take a turn to a staircase and slowly climb it. Having called over Nicole to help find Gloria, the house painter would reach out to hug her, much to Gloria’s protest.

“Oh thank goodness, we’re sooo lucky to have found you so quickly!”

“Please... let go... I can feel your hands squeezing on my back rolls...”

“Oh, sorry...” Blushing heavily, Chalchi would let go of Gloria, as the latter would begin searching through her pockets. After a bit of looking, Gloria would quickly find what she was looking for, and show the two the golden utensils she had found.

“Okay, so that place I found was some sort of ballroom, and alongside some weird statue I found these golden utensils, 10 of them in all! They should serve well to sell for renovations!”

Being given the golden whisk by Gloria, Nicole would look at it, a bit of a frown on her face as she would examine it. “Uh Gloria, I get that these are probably expensive, but aside from me being right about the place being not that good, it doesn’t exactly feel right to sell what could be forgotten heirlooms...”

“I mean,” Gloria would say, as she would slowly put back the other utensils into her pockets, “it’s not like they need them anymore... They’re long and gone, and these boys should probably come with the house considering we found them inside of it!”

“I know... I just don’t feel right... morally that is.”

Thinking about things, Gloria would eventually take the whisk back from Nicole, before sighing. “Ugh, fine... I’ll keep them for now. But if we need any extra payment for this manor, these babies going on auction...”

Putting the whisk away last, Gloria and the others would continue to look through the manor, this time a bit more cautious after Gloria’s earlier fall from the third floor to the second. As they would search the rest of the manor, they would find that the wood would certainly need to be replaced and repaired, and the floors reinforced with iron to prevent another floor.

With the manor now fully checked, Gloria would leave Sandra and Chalchi to come up with renovating the manor, and Nicole to go home, a bit of worry in her face about Gloria’s continued esteem for the manor. Heading back home, she would slowly put away the golden utensils in a safe place, but keeping a hold of the whisk.

“Hmm... I’ll keep the rest away, but I may have some use for you. I’ve been needing a new whisk anyway, so this will probably serve me well... not to mention maybe it’ll serve as a good status symbol for my empire...”

Making a quick hop into her kitchen, Gloria would put the whisk away, grabbing the old rusty whisk she was replacing and throwing it away. Having swapped things out, the chef would wander off to her bedroom, and prepare for the upcoming months.

After all, these renovations were gonna be really long and tough...
125311.title: The Shrine of Marianne Malanor

choices: After a few days, Gloria would meet with Agata for menu prepping.
As Gloria plans the food, Sandra and Chalchi renovate the manor.
Continuing to have hunger pangs every night, Gloria decides to talk with Nicole.
1253113.content:
“Gah!”

It would be around midnight the day after she had searched through the Malanor Mansion, and Gloria would wake up from a heavy nightmare with a startling jump. She had a horrifying dream about the manor, where she could have sworn to see Marianne herself in her dreams, and she was somewhat frightened from what she saw.

Breathing in and out a bit, Gloria would try to calm herself down, as she would try to forget about the dream. “Calm down, girl. It’s just a bad dream, it’s not like-“

*gruuumble*

“Ugh, I could use a snack...”

Slowly rising out from bed, Gloria would rise up from bed as she would rub her stomach. She had gone to bed after a full course meal that was heavy even for her, yet as she would feel her stomach, it felt completely soft, as if she hadn’t eaten at all. Could she have digested it all in such a short timeframe?

Before she could think any more however, Gloria would feel a grumble coming from her stomach, causing her to lose track as she would reach in to her fridge. Pulling out some leftovers she had planned to eat in the morning, she would place them on the kitchen counter and begin to reach in and open them.

Moving to grab a spoon, Gloria would come back and open up a large container of potatoes before digging in. She would dig through the potatoes with quick abandon, swallowing it all as her stomach would continue growling for more, still not satisfied with what it has consumed so far.

Moving on to the next two tins, Gloria would open both of them to reveal some assorted slices of meatloaf. Feeling her stomach growl heavier than she ever had before. Not even bothering with grabbing a fork as her hunger would overtake her, Gloria would simply grab the pieces, stuffing it down her maw with quick abandon.

This would continue through the next hour, as Gloria would clear through the rest of the leftovers as well as a carton of ice cream, before slumping with her back facing against the fridge. Rubbing her belly, now overly stuffed, the chef would feel a bit of lethargy overcome her, the same kind one would feel if one had eaten a Thanksgiving feast.

“Ugh... too full... really shouldn’t have... eaten that last bit of ice cream...”

Eventually, Gloria would slump into a small food coma, slumping to the ground as she would sleep through the rest of the night. As night would change into day, she would eventually wake up as the sun would shine in her face, with a big yawn.

“*yaaawn* Ah, that felt much better... wait, why am I in the kitchen?”

Rising up to her feet, Gloria could barely remember a thing from last night, only remembering a intense feeling of hunger. Rising up, the first thing she would feel would be her own stomach, looking the slightest bit bigger while also still somewhat firm from last night. Feeling around, she could even feel a few stretch marks, something that has never happened to her before.

Eventually, as she would reach up to a standing position, Gloria would see the empty containers and carton, causing her to fully remember what had happened. Letting out a small belch, the chef would grab the containers before throwing them in the sink.

“Now I remember... I’ll just clean these later, I got some more important things to do now that I’m up anyway...”

Making herself a quick and light pancake breakfast due to still feeling a bit full from what had happened last night, Gloria would pull out her phone, and slowly pull out a list of all of her contacts and chefs for the Manor. It would be a while before she would open the place, but she would still be thinking about chefs as she would look through.

Thinking through, Gloria would eventually settle on hiring mostly new chefs, though with a addition of a chef from one of her first restaurants, the Henhouse Kitchen, by the name of Agata. Agata was a baker who was Gloria’s senior of around 3 years, and around the more heavyset end of her employees, being heavier than even Nicole yet still staying barely under the 300 pound threshold. With the plans for the Manor’s menu to be focused on sweets and baked dinners, she was the perfect chef to manage the place.

Calling Agata, Gloria would wait for the rotund Italian chef to answer the call first. “Hey Gloria, what you want?”

“Hey Agata, I am building a new restaurant and hiring new staff to work there. Your gonna be promoted to manager of this new place, so get ready to pack and move to Malanor Manor... or whatever we call the restaurant.”

“A promotion?” Agata would say, a worried tone coming from her voice. “Are you really sure? What about the others? You expect them to be good without me?”

“Oh, I’m sure they’ll be fine! They’ve been learning a lot from you, so I’m sure they’ll be okay without you helping guide them at every step.”

“Really? I guess I could come then... oh! In that case, do you think I could come over later this week to help come up with a menu?”

“Yeah, of course. You’ll be the head chef on top of being manager, so you’ll have full power over it.”

“Cool! I’ll come over this Thursday, and bring some samples for you to eat! I’m sure you’ll enjoy them!”

“Good, I’ll see you then.”

*klick*
1253113.title: A Burgeoning Curse...

choices: Gina Bianchi - Fresh faced Italian belle
Blake Moffatt - Smouldering ex, smouldering business
Lacey Truman - Sugar coated British bitch
Grant McCray - Customer orientated enabler
Natalie ‘Nat’ Greenway - An expanded Elusive Universe option
13.content:
Before Gloria could truly come to terms with what her day entailed, the heavy double doors of her luxurious office swung open as the hurried clicking of heels tore into Gloria’s inner sanctum.

“Miss Vandergast! Miss Vandergast!” wailed the almost shrill voice of Gloria’s young assistant, Danni, cutting through the sublime victory that the Kitchen Boss had just secured.

“Oh don’t be so dramatic Danni, it is first thing in a morning dear”
Despite her usual temperament that had been flexed against the unprepared supplier on the phone, Gloria had a soft spot for the young girl that dutifully organised the basic day to day scheduling of her business and was often more teasing than cruel to the blonde assistant.

“Sorry Miss, it’s just...we have a problem” panted Danni as she halted her trot opposite Gloria with a folder clutched tightly to her button shirt-clad chest.

“It can’t be that bad Danni, what’s happened?”
The Kitchen Boss was not keen on the direction this seemed to be taking and adopted a far more imposing position within her executive chair ahead of the upcoming explaination.

*****

“That absolute bitch!” spat Gloria with rage in her eyes and an angry flush coating her face and cleavage.

“I’m sorry Miss Vandergast” repeated Danni for the umpteenth time as the unfortunate information had sunk in.

“So let me get this straight, this FORMER employee was ACTUALLY a spy and her SUPPOSED Human Resources complaint was her escape plan because she’s been conducting INDUSTRIAL ESPIONAGE?!”

Danni could only nod out of fear as her boss seemed to rise further from her perch far beyond her short height would suggest.

“This is a DISGRACE!” screamed Gloria as the lavish fittings of her office rattled from the volume “I paid her wages for 6 MONTHS!”

Danni nodded again just hoping to ride out the storm. She had seen Gloria get angry before but this was a whole new level. After all, no one had ever gone after the Kitchen Boss’s empire like this.

“Who sent her?” asked Gloria with a terrifying calm after her outburst.

“Well it’s not entirely clear at this point...” began Danni.

“You must have some idea Danni” snapped Gloria sharply as she heavily dumped herself back into her desk chair which squeaked quietly under her girth.

Instinctively Danni almost tossed the folder she was holding towards her boss as a collection of papers slid from the confines of the wallet.

“Those are the most likely suspects” muttered Danni quickly as she looked at her shoes wishing dearly to be dismissed, a thought that appeared to be read by her senior.

“Go, I’ll call if I need you” retorted Gloria with her characteristic snark as she opened the folder and began to flick through the pages presented to her.
Danni meanwhile turned tail and made her escape even faster than her initial entry.

*****

With a heavy sigh and five piles of varying thicknesses before her, Gloria was left with a decision she certainly hadn’t expected for the day.

“Well every one of these has a reason to hate me” admitted Gloria, slightly self gratifying, as she glanced over each pile “ok, let’s get started”

*****

First up was an admittedly new player but one backed by some serious culinary muscle.

Gina Bianchi was the youngest daughter of the legendary Bianchi family who had dominated the Italian scene for decades and, with Gina’s youthful flair, were on the rise once again.

A wild combination of Gina’s raw talent in the kitchen bringing American and Mexican fusion to family tradition and the young Italian beauty’s sex appeal driven social media presence was bringing in enormous crowds way beyond what her predecessors could have imagined.

Her food was delicious, which Gloria knew first hand, her stunning olive tanned physique, natural beauty and flirtatious friendliness had done wonders for the chain of restaurants her family owned.
Fired up as she was though, Gloria couldn’t help but think that Gina was a ticking time bomb of genetics as every Bianchi woman was eventually cursed with a metabolic crash and calorie soaked hips to boot.
Even Gina’s older sister Isabella, a senior by three years, had blimped at the hands of her new husband and her own sisters cooking.

Next was a face that Gloria did not want to see which, for anyone unfamiliar with the chiseled features and roguish stubble, was easier said than done under the red scrawls of derogatory sketches over the photo.

Blake Moffatt had been Gloria’s long term boyfriend in college and they had shared a like minded ambition to develop a restaurant franchise to rule the eatery district together.

Sadly though Blake had other ideas and, just before graduation, tossed Gloria to the side as his own greed and ambition outgrew his romance.
That emotional kicking only motivated Gloria to make her own way in the world though and, despite her extra romantic weight, was well on the way to succeed if not for the latest espionage attempt against her.

Opting to flip Blake’s photo over having seen enough of her former flame’s face for one day, Gloria moved to her third pile which wrenched an equally disgusted groan from the dark haired entrepreneur’s lips

“Lacey Lacey Lacey...”

That was a bitch that could do some damage.

Lacey Truman was, unlike the rest of the people on Gloria’s desk, less of a restaurateur and more of an independent baker.
British born and a dab hand in the kitchen, Lacey was just as sweet as the mountains of confections she produced every day for an avid customer base who worshipped the ground the Lindy Bop styled chick walked on.

Very few knew how much of a charade the cutesy style and sickly sweet attitude was though as a wickedly savage business woman hid beneath who was rumoured to be running a labour intensive ‘apprentice scheme’ that was more demoralising than beneficial for the young girls she took on.

Lacey certainly controlled her audience though with the product she was selling, not to mention her body positive spin she had taken on since packing on some pounds herself forming a fluffy hourglass figure over time.

Feeling her anger starting build as she stared into that sickening chubby smile painted across the brunette’s face behind an outstretched tray of muffins, Gloria moved onto her next suspect who was something of a curveball.

Grant McCray was not the ruthless businessman the others were and certainly no Gloria for that matter.
Rather, Grant had his own little corner of the foodee empire that catered to a friendly audience that just wanted to eat and enjoy some good food,

He was good at what he did and certainly fit the stereotype advising against skinny chefs but there was little form to suggest he would have a reason to directly strike out against Gloria.

That said Gloria, and most everybody on the desk before her, were well aware of Grant’s...preferences...towards larger women and a larger client base could play into that...
Gloria was not entirely convinced though.

This brought Gloria to her final, and thickest, pile of papers.

Whilst Gina Bianchi was new on the scene, the business and family was at least familiar to Gloria.
The fifth individual however was a complete dark horse.

“Natalie Greenway?” muttered Gloria under her breath as she flicked through the papers which were, admittedly, mostly screen grabs from various social media pages showing a dark haired girl at college with blazing shoulder and thigh tattoos glimmering outside bits of clothing.

As she continued to flick through photos Gloria began to recognise details, namely the arrival of a club that she vaguely remembered as well as a stream of job adverts asking for chefs and waiting staff fo said club.

Amongst the photos a few faces began to pop out to Gloria too, including a motherly figured blonde with glasses, a pair of bottom heavy red headed twins and a very athletic black girl with a stern look.
