“What a strange group...” thought Gloria as she came to the end of the pages, making a mental note that Natalie did appear to slowly thicken up across the board as the dates raced closer to the present.
Although she’d never heard of this ‘Nat’ before, Gloria could sense ambition and that was dangerous for business.

With another audible sigh, Gloria leant back in her chair as she began to ponder the situation.

“Five suspects...but who’s out to get me?”
13.title: War is hell and business is ruthless

choices: Gina sets in motion a plan to out manoeuvre Gloria’s expansion plans
Gloria makes the first move
Business can wait, Gina has breakfast with Isabella the following morning
A food critic award up for grabs pits both Kitchen Bosses against each other.
131.content:
“Thank you everybody for coming tonight” bellowed Marlon Bianchi over the gentle hum of chatter buzzing around the table before him “it’s a pleasure to have you all here celebrating with our sweet Gina”

As the reigning patriarch of the Bianchi family it had been a tough decision the day he opted to hand over the family restaurant empire to his youngest daughter.
Marlon had questioned whether Gina was ready at just 25 years old and if she truly understood how cut throat the industry really was but the spunky Italian girl had taken it in her stride.

That had been six months back now and the business was going from strength to strength and nobody was more proud than Marlon himself.
“And here she comes now!” beamed the family head as his prodigal daughter hip checked the swinging kitchen door, yet another platter of food cradled in one arm as Gina slid her phone into the back pocket of her jeans as she walked towards her applauding family.

“Oh papa, stop” cooed Gina with an almost blush “everybody eat before it goes cold”

“Always the worker” chuckled Marlon as he took his seat whilst Gina sidled round him to place the meat loaded platter amongst dishes of spiced rice, rich creamy hand made pastas and tortillas as well as multiple freshly cooked pizzas according to her family’s dough recipe.

“Sit down Gina, you’ve cooked us a feast” smiled her mother, Sofia, as she watched her daughter dote over various members of the family.

“I will mama, just...” started Gina as she came up behind her sister Isabella with an arm around her shoulders.

“Sit Gina” insisted her mother with a stern look and a hand instinctively shooting to a motherly hip.

“Yes mama” relented Gina as she took the empty seat next to Isabella, contented that everybody was filling their plates.

“You’ve done amazing sis, enjoy it” purred Isabella as she hungrily filled her plate, eating as much as she placed before it reached the plate.

“Thanks Iz” smiled Gina in return, placing a hand on her sister’s plush thigh.

With only three years between them you would expect Gina and Isabella to share a striking resemblance but those three years and a marriage on Isabella’s side had made quite the difference.
See, the female Bianchi genes have quite a unique trait in that at some point or another their metabolisms fade to nothing and all that Italian home cooking takes hold of the hips and never lets go.

So whilst Gina was quite the stunner with a svelte olive tinted figure that had served purpose in her aggressive social media presence in advertising the restaurant chain since she had taken charge of it with a large part of her client base possessing a healthy attraction to the young chef, Isabella was much more motherly in appearance.

To be more precise Isabella was knocking on the gates of 300lbs that very night in no small part to her doting husband and Gina’s own cooking which was all firmly settled around her hips and ass, not to mention the plump middle that was sprouting forth too.

Facially the sisters were easily linked with Isabella remaining thin around the cheeks and neck but that was where the physical similarities ended.
The elders weight gain did nothing to damage their relationship though and they were inseparable through each other’s successes.

“A toast” declared Marlon from the head of the table “to the next generation”

“Oh god” muttered Isabella under her breath as the entire family looked to her and Gina.

“Just breath, it’ll be over soon” teased Gina as the glasses rose around them.

“To the next generation” came the chorus of voices before a unified drink happened as the younger girls laughed to themselves.

“Thank you everybody” began Gina, rising from her seat at that point with a delicate dab at the corner of her lips with her napkin “I wouldn’t be where I am now if it wasn’t for you all here and I hope to do you proud in the years to come”

There was a delicate rumble of acknowledgment and clapping at the sentiment as Gina smiled at the crowd of family surrounding her, instinctively putting a hand to the vibrating phone in her tight jeans pocket, knowing better than to pull it out.

“Now, everybody eat and enjoy” announced Gina as she returned to her seat, delicate decorum resuming as she took up her cutlery again in sync with the people around her.

“You not going to say anything sis?” teased Gina again, side glancing Isabella who was far too busy practically deep throating a self constructed burrito loaded with Mexican spiced meatballs and rice.

“Huh? No way” grunted Isabella around a mouthful of food, tossing thick black curls from her face as she did.

“Really? No, like, baby news or something?” Gina continued to tease, knowing her sister wasn’t pregnant nor even trying.

The senior Bianchi sister nearly did a spit take at that.
“Ha, I’m just enjoying being married to an awesome guy right now” replied Isabella with a dreamy smile “think you’re closer to popping the baby news than me sis”

“Yeah right” smirked Gina as she delicately dug in to her food.

“Well maybe not but it’d make a change to see you pop a button or two from those skinny jeans of yours” teased Isabella, returning to her food with gusto.

*****

‘Phew, Isabella might get that popped button sooner than she thinks if I’m not careful’ moaned Gina to herself as she unlocked the door to her apartment and flicked the hallway light on.

It was past midnight after a long night of celebrating the family’s new found success and Gina was ready to be free of day clothes and curled up in bed.
Not to mention she had some things to deal with.

Casually discarding her boots by the door and sliding her phone from within her jeans pocket, Gina trotted towards her bedroom and began reading over her messages.
Most of them were standard social media notifications with most of them gushing over either pictures of Gina herself or the numerous updates about the restaurants.
One though, an email to be specific, caught her eye.

The subject line simply read ‘GV’ and was from a garbled address that Gina was familiar with.
It hadn’t been easy getting Fiona into Gloria Vandergast’s operation Gina knew something had to be done to get an edge on her biggest competition.
After all her father had spent long enough lecturing her on the ruthless nature of business so she had to put the lesson to good use.

The email was a simple link that led to an encrypted drop box containing a combination of audio files, videos, photos and a document containing brief notes about what the rest of the files were.
Most of it would have to wait until the morning but Gina had enough energy in her to skim through the notes to get the gist of what Fiona had found whilst undercover.

Most of it was standard ‘chef stuff’ in the form of new recipes that Gloria had concocted which were too dangerous to risk copying, Vandergast would know for sure then, but most promising was Gloria’s expansion plans for her empire.

‘Oh boy, that could be a problem’ thought Gina as she discarded her phone along with the rest of her clothes aside from her underwear as she disappeared into the soft bedding she had been craving for the past few hours, wriggling her narrow hips in between the sheets.

‘I’ll find a way’ Gina told herself ‘papa always said that Bianchi’s find a way’
The reassuring thoughts were the last thing crossing her mind as she fell asleep, her business mind subconsciously ticking away as she fell into a gentle sleep.
131.title: Gina Bianchi - Fresh faced Italian belle

choices: Gloria has Gina shadowed, trying to uncover her secrets.
Spiteful about the failed Huerta deal, Gloria sabotages the re-opening.
It's time for Gina to be knocked down a peg; her metabolism, that is.
No matter the scheme, Gina has a swift solution to Gloria's acts of vengeance.
1311.content:
After a good night's sleep following the big announcement dinner of her fully inheriting the Bianchi family enterprises, Gina had dreamt up some schemes to block Gloria Vandergast's efforts while furthering the family's brand. Partial to Turkish coffee when she was brainstorming big plans, Gina placed a few calls while her coffee warmed.

"Ciao, Fiona," Gina greeted to her co-conspirator. "Yes, I know you said no further calls but don't worry, I'm calling from a burner phone. I wanted to ask you about those properties listed in the files. Just how much was her bid?"

After some brief minutes of terse talk, Gina had the further intel she needed as her coffee had finished brewing. Dressed only in her bathrobe, the Italian belle took her coffee out to her apartment's balcony overlooking the central courtyard. The Mediterranean fresco design and roof tiles of the apartment complex had reminded Gina of her family's ancestral home when she was first looking for her own home. She took aim with the burner phone and hurled it into the courtyard fountain.

"Oops, ~sorry Fernando~" she giggled to herself, as the groundskeeper would have to fish that out at some point. Reclining on her patio furniture, Gina savored a sip of her strong coffee as she made a string of other calls throughout the morning.

"Hi Papa!" Went the first call. "I know it might be gauche talking business so soon, but I wanted to make sure we had the funds set aside for that planned expansion. I think we should move up the timing..." The Bianchi holdings, while renowned, were surprisingly small compared to the fast franchising moving of Gina's generation. There was the historic original family restaurant, the second location much like the first, the third where Gina had started out cooking with her fusion cuisines, and the fourth was the eatery of an old town grocery store. Marlon, one for family and communal ties, had been unsure about aggressive expansion of the family restaurants but Gina knew just how to twist him around her finger.

"Heeeeey Iz~!" Went the second call. "Aww, your fat pants aren't fitting? I'm shocked," she teased, as her sister Izabella had been just under 300 lbs before the amount she had eaten last night. "Listen, sis, we can talk sisterly woes later, I've got a question for your hubby Benito. Hush, I wouldn't dream of it, you know he loves every fiber of your being! Enough teasing, I actually wanted to ask about his friends on the building contractors' Union..." Benito, Izabella's darling husband, certainly had a hand in her growing size beyond the family's cooking. As a construction worker, after a hard day of working with boards, nails, I-beams and concrete, he loved coming home to a plush wife. The sisters were so close that they teased each other relentlessly and were openly candid about their respective love lives.

"Hello, is this the Huerta residence?" Went the third call. "I'm calling about your recent sale. My name is Gina Bianchi. Yes, Marlon Bianchi's daughter, I've recently picked up the reigns of the family business...oh, your grandkids were at the event? Wonderful! Family is important to me, and I've heard yours is in a spot of trouble..."


Gloria Vandergast did not need this week to continue.

In the aftermath of the corporate spy discovery, Gloria had yet to determine who had wronged her and nothing appeared to be going right. She had her hunches but no proof, just a series of conveniently timed obstacles to the growth of her restaurant empire. Monday's revelation was followed by bad news on Tuesday; Gloria had been out-bid in a prime land scale from the city recently commercializing some undeveloped land. Wednesday's woe had the workforce renovating her original bistro after an 'accidental' fire (ostensibly for insurance purposes) walk-out on strike as she apparently hadn't filled the right union paperwork.

Thursday was proving to be just as terrible. For Gloria's diet, that is. The stress of finding her plans for expansion foiled, obviously the work of whoever got that stooge hired at main restaurant for half a year, had gotten Gloria on a fast food kick. No, she wasn't buying fast food, she was making it!

Gloria had never 'eaten out' since she she had become a big name chef, unless it was for business reasons like sussing out the competition. Becoming a bit of a snob about her food, she wouldn't eat anything she didn't have a hand in nor that of her own talent. So as she was driving her cobalt Jaguar out to the site of her next meeting, wearing her designer sunglasses and a navy suit, she was eating her concoction of a Wendy's Baconator combo meal. That being: a triple patty gourmet burger with jalapeño bacon and garlic aioli mayo; truffle oil double-crunch fries with rosemary; and a horchata-milkshake. The 84 oz Pepsi was store bought.

"At least I've still got the Huerta property in my clutches," Gloria mused as she parked her car, inhaling the last bite of her burger. Crushing the wrapper she had gotten for it, Gloria checked herself in the mirror for any signs of spilled food on her suit. Seeing none and nursing a long swig of horchata-shake, she got out of the car with a grunt.

Casa Su Vecindario was a mom and pop Mexican restaurant that had fallen on hard times, owing to an unfavorable market shift. Most of the old neighborhood had dried up as department brand stores and a mega mall off the interstate drew the crowds out of the area. The family that ran the restaurant, the Huertas, could no longer afford to run the restaurant given a price hike in ingredients. Having discovered this fruitful opportunity, Gloria had intimidated the Huertas into a private sale. What she promised them was an absolute steal from her perspective. She had ambitions for an experimental Tex-Mex joint to replace it, heavy emphasis on the Tex.

"Hola, Señor Huerta," Gloria greeted, eager to get the papers signed over. Upon removing her sunglasses as she entered the empty restaurant, there was an unexpected fresh face standing next to the aged owners.

"Ah, so you're Gloria Vandergast!" Gina Bianchi had opted for a low cut but tasteful pinstripe business dress that accentuated her sleek, sexy curves. The olive-skinned Italian bombshell was all smiles to the heftier Gloria's simmering hatred.

"Gina Bianchi," Gloria returned the false pleasantries. "Heard you just stepped up into the big leagues."

"Hmm, I've seen bigger," Gina had a private chuckle at the thought of how her sister outweighed the dumpy physiqued Gloria by a hundred pounds.

Feeling insulted, Gloria addressed Oscar Huerta. "If she's offering to buy your place, remember that she's young blood to the game. I doubt without her family's constant support she'll keep a new restaurant open more than two years."

"Oh, I'm not trying to buy Casa Su Vecindario," Gina said. "I'm the Huerta family's new business partner."

That caught Gloria completely off guard. "What?"

Oscar nodded along with Gina's assessment. "Senoirta Bianchi wanted to sponsor the restaurant in trust. Our grandchildren have been inspired by her recent success and want to keep the restaurant in the family."

"And I provide them some fresh recipes and initial support staff until they create their own crew," Gina smiled. Latin cuisine was her favorite after that of her Italian heritage, a cornerstone of her Americana inspired fusion dishes. "So we figured our first order of business would be telling you in person that the Huerta family rejects your offer of sale."

Gloria had just enough composure to leave the disastrous meeting on civil terms, although she was all rants and rage when she returned to her Jaguar. Stuffing fries into her face, Gloria placed a call to her home office.

"It was the Bianchi Bitch!" Gloria fumed, having found her saboteur. "I want everything that can be dug up on her unearthed! I want horrid reviews on all her eateries! I want her head on a pike!" The last demand was hopefully hyperbole. "She's going to to rue the day she messed with Gloria Vandergast! SHIT!"

Added to Thursday's troubles, Gloria now had to contend with getting Pepsi stains out of her car mats.
1311.title: It's Less What You Know But Who You Know

choices: Construction begins on Gina’s mega restaurant
Having suitably rattled Gloria, Gina eases into complacency
With the right help the Huerta’s prove valuable business partners
If she can't sabotage Gina, Gloria will show her up with an even bigger project.
13114.content:
“Thank you Senorita Bianchi, I could never stand that woman” sighed Oscar with a rush of relief.

“Oh Senior Huerta” chuckled Gina placing a reassuring hand on her new business partner’s shoulder “she won’t be bothering you or your family anymore”

“We can’t thank you enough” beamed Alejandra Huerta “our los nietos will know what you have done for them this day”

Gina smiled with a modest blush warming her cheeks.
“No no, I’m just here to help a family in the community and I look forward to our partnership”

“Then a toast” insisted Oscar as he shot into the back room.

“Well, maybe just one” relented Gina with a grin “but first could I get a photo with you both?”
The question came with a smooth draw of her phone from inside the small clutch bag she had at her hip, the text for the post she would use to accompany the photo she was about to take already ready.

*****

“DANNI! Get me Vinnie and SOMETHING sweet!”

Gloria was pissed.
Gina was a slip of a girl with no business experience compared to Gloria and here she was stealing her business? This was unacceptable and things were going to get ugly from here on out.

“He...here you go Miss Vander...” stammered Danni stretching out an index card as well as a whole cream cake on a crystal stand.
The assistant found herself cut off brutally as Gloria snatched both items from her, slamming the index card down whilst grabbing a folk from her desk drawer that violently began stabbing into the two tiered sponge.

“Get out” spat Gloria around a mouthful of half chewed cake, her none eating hand punching numbers into her desk phone which began ringing as Danni scampered away.

“Vincent Scrowler, food critic”

“Bianchi, tomorrow” spat Gloria with no hint of pleasantries.

“Ahh, Miss Vandergast, a pleasure as always” drawled Vincent somewhere between sarcasm and irritation.
He’d suffered enough ball bustings at the hands of Miss Vandergast since she’d emerged on the scene but she had paid him handsomely over the years which was one of the few things maintaining their awkward quote on quote working relationship.

“I don’t want to hear it” scoffed Gloria through bulging cake filled cheeks “just go and EVISCERATE the girl TOMORROW”

“Hmm” sighed Vincent as he adjusted the grip on his phone “I’ll do my job”

*****

“Thanks for dining with us tonight” purred Gina as she followed a sweet couple out the door, wiping her hands on her apron as she did.

“It was amazing” gushed the woman “you’re food is so delicious, my husband will need his pants letting out soon”

“Well you would say that seeing as yours already HAVE been let out” teased the aforementioned husband as he posted a sizeable tip into the jar by the door.

“You’re so sweet, see you soon!” chirped Gina as she held the door, waving the couple off.
As Gina turned to head back to the kitchen the antique bell rang against the top of the door to which the young Italian instinctively span around.

“Welcome to our restau...oh Mr Scrowler, please come in” ushered Gina “what do I owe this honour tonight?”

“Good evening Miss Bianchi” returned Vincent as he hung his jacket by the door “if I could just have a table for one this evening that would be wonderful”

“This way Mr Scrowler” smiled Gina, beckoning the tall critic into the restaurant.
Although Gina had never personally met Vincent Scrowler his reputation proceeded him. Aside from his prolific critics column her father had warned her against the often cutting food critic and how his written words had ended chef’s with years of experience.
Six months in seemed like a perfect time to cross that bridge if not coincidental given her meeting with Gloria the day before.

As Gina seated Vincent in a quiet corner of the restaurant she stepped herself in front of the critic where his eyes immediately drew to the young woman’s exposed middle where her shirt was tied up under her breasts.

“What can I get for you ton...”

“Is that dress code fitting of health regulations in this city Miss Bianchi?”

“Oh erm yes, we’re fully checked out here, I actually have a really good relationship with...”

“Uh huh, well in that case I will have some of that signature Bianchi pasta” retorted Vincent, not even glancing over a menu.

“Coming right up Signore” smiled Gina as she practically ran back to the kitchen, a flash of panic on her face.

*****

“Shit Iz, Scrowler is here!” hissed Gina into her phone, ducked behind the fridges.

“Who?” grunted Isabella clearly eating.

“The food critic papa always spoke about!”

“Oh...oh! You ok?” swallowed Isabella realising the gravity of the situation.

“No, I’m freaking out and he wants papa’s special pasta!” hissed Gina again, glancing round as her kitchen continued to work busily around her.

“Well then make it, you can do that dish blindfolded” reassured Isabella.

“Yeah I guess...it’s got to be that Vandergast woman though” resigned Gina, wondering if she’d pushed too far.

“Yeah? Well screw her, you got this!” urged the older sister “besides, Benito has you covered with the other plan so she can’t touch that, just cook your little ass off tonight”

Gina breathed heavily before standing up straight.
“Yeah, you’re right, thanks Iz”

“You bet, now go make some pasta”

With a small smirk Gina shoved her phone away and, with a determined stride untied her shirt allowing it to tumble back over her olive tanned stomach as she swiped up a pan.

*****

“Good morning Danni” purred Gloria as she strutted past her assistant, pushing her office doors open with a smile in her face.

“Good...morning?”
Danni was confused, the sudden mood change with her boss was unsettling after the days of foul mood but then, it was Wednesday which everybody in the food industry knew was Scrowler’s Scrawlings day; Vincent Scrowler’s food critic column.

“Danni darling, has the paper come in yet?” cooed Gloria from her desk, her seat groaning softly.

“Erm, yes! Yes it has” replied Danni leaping from her own desk with the smoothly folded paper in her hand.

“How wonderful” purred Gloria as she gleefully stared at the paper.

“Here you go Miss” Danni cautiously offered as she placed the paper on the desk before her boss “shall I get your morning coffee now?”

“Oh yes please Danni” replied Gloria, not really listening as she started flicking through the paper.

Danni walked away with an almost smile on her slightly chubby face, was today going to be a good day?

The young assistant had been gone all of 3 minutes and 52 seconds when that estimation had gone down the pan.

“That absolute waste of...oh I’m gonna...where’s my...DANNI!”

“Miss Vandergast?” meekly asked Danni with a coffee cup in her hand.

Don’t you Miss...just listen to this” spat Gloria, eyes wide and wild.

‘Despite a sudden and unexpected change in leadership, the Bianchi chain has not lost any of its family orientated charm. Whilst Gina Bianchi, the new ‘don’ of the chain, may be slightly unconventional in her business model which anybody on social media and other such modern outlets will know and attest to, she has not lost what was at the heart of the family restaurants; food. Whilst I may spend hours waxing lyrical about the delectable culinary delights I was honoured with during my visit I shall do no such thing and instead urge any and everyone to visit and enjoy what young Gina Bianchi has to offer.
I await the opportunity to return as well as visit Miss Bianchi’s newest venture due to open soon’

“Well that’s not...” started Danni before being screamed into silence.

“HE WAS SUPPOSED TO DESTROY HER!” screamed Gloria with a pulsating vein in her forehead “get him on the phone now!”

Danni nodded fearfully as she disappeared back to her desk to make the call.
Still fuming, Gloria’s eyes snapped to the phone when it rang before snatching it up.

“What are you playing at! I asked you to tear her a new one and THIS is what I get?”

“Good morning Gloria” sneered Vincent with genuine disdain this time.

“Don’t you...hngh...and why have you endorsed her new venture?!”

“Because she deserved it Gloria and I advise you to leave the girl alone” retorted Vincent “she is a talent that you can’t bully out”

“Oh you just wa...”

BEEEEP

“Son of a...what Danni?!”

Danni nearly jumped out of her skin as her boss turned on her so viciously.
“Th...the...the mail is here” declared the blonde as she dropped it down on the desk.

A glare was enough to send Danni scuttling out as Gloria tore open the large envelope and yanked the documents out.
It was a planning application, that was clear, but a delicately hand written note was stuck to the first page.

‘You didn’t think the Huerta’s were my only plan did you? GB X’

Gloria angrily flicked through the pages to see a neo-classical masterpiece of architecture with high occupancy and a state of the art kitchen.
Practically frothing at the mouth Gloria reached the back page where another note was stuck with a picture attached with Gina shaking hands with an unknown individual.

‘By the way, this is all legit so don’t even think about it’

“Mother FU...”
13114.title: One step forward, another Italian step forward

choices: Gloria concocts a plan to undermine Gina’s sex appeal driven social media
Celebrations are in order as Gina’s new restaurant opens
Isabella takes a leaf out of Gina’s book and...cooks?
131142.content:
“God I wish I could have seen her face when she read that note!” laughed Isabella, her plush gut bouncing as she did.

“Yeah me too” smirked her husband Benito, casually side eyeing his wife’s jiggling frame “that took some guts facing up to her like that Gina”

Gina blushed as she placed a platter of spiced meats onto the table before her sister and brother in law before she took her own seat, surrounded by rices, two types of pasta, a large serving dish of dauphinois potatoes as well as freshly baked garlic breads and tortillas.

“Seriously Gina, you did good fronting up to that witch” complimented Isabella as she loaded up what was inevitably the first of many plates that night “and not to mention...oh my god, this is amazing!”

Benito was also starting on a much smaller plate and was far more reserved than his corpulent wife but was just as appreciative as he softly smiled from the second the first mouthful hit his tongue.

“Oh stop you two” Gina insisted as she tucked in to her own food “this is just practice, nothing special”

“Please Gina, your practice dishes put anybody else to shame in this city” insisted Isabella through a repeatedly replenished mouthful “although that is...mhm, so good...not to say you can stop practicing here”

Despite the tease Gina had a long standing arrangement that she would visit her sister at least once a week which would give her chance to trial new recipes in a more friendly atmosphere before taking them to the family restaurant.
That said, the fact such visits had increased to twice a week since Isabella’s wedding with Benito often reminding the younger Bianchi she was welcome whenever she wanted had only benefitted both sisters.

On one hand Gina got to experiment more and more which had undoubtedly contributed to her rapid rise in success whilst Isabella had a blinding excuse to refresh her wardrobe regularly as her figure ballooned out of any and all clothing at the hands of her sister’s irresistible cooking in no time at all.

“I think what your sister is trying to say is that your set to make some huge leaps forward as a business woman and you shouldn’t feel obligated to be spending all your time here” reassured Benito with a glint in his eyes as Isabella swatted at him whilst complaining through a full mouth that she swallowed awkwardly.

“Shut up babe, I’d starve if Gina stopped coming over” moaned Isabella with almost genuine concern painted across her comparably slender facial features.

“Somehow I doubt that sis” teased Gina with a forkful to her lips as she watched Benito playfully squeeze Isabella’s belly through her straining blouse.
It was always sweet seeing the married couple interact as well as the love that oozed from them whenever they were together. Gina was jealous of the genuine nature of her sister’s marriage whilst her own love life was usually more superficial with men usually just fawning over her looks.
Whilst not interested in gaining weight, and certainly not to the extent that Izzy had, the couple before her certainly sold the concept as appealing.

*****

The night rolled on as the three people chatted and ate and drank a suitably copious amount of wine which inevitably led to giddiness.

“Imagine that Gloria woman when you push her out of the industry Gina” mused Isabella, still putting away plates of food long after Gina and Benito had finished eating.
The fact she’d had to undo her belt and allow her bulging belly to rest free of denim on her lap suggested Isabella herself maybe should stop but her appetite ran as deep as Gina’s culinary talent.

“We’ll see Iz” purred Gina having reclined in her chair to enjoy her wine “it’s still early days yet”

“So cynical, you know Gina you should really BUUUURP” belched Isabella, causing her to flush slightly as a hand shot to her belly, softly sinking into the sun kissed skin.

“Oh really?” teased Gina, trying not to laugh by biting her lip whilst Benito snuggled against his wife, kissing her cheek.

“Oh shush, like you’ve never burped before” complained Isabella as she rested against Benito, taking his hand and placing it on her belly.

“Well you’ve got me there” admitted Gina as she continued to sip on her wine “seriously though, you think I can beat Gloria? Even when she tried to get us shut down with Scrowler?”

“Clearly didn’t work though right?” replied Benito having read the review the critic had written after his impromptu visit, which had been distributed around as many colleagues and clients the labourer could get it to.

“I suppose” murmured Gina as she recalled the glowing endorsement Scrowler had given.

“Besides, all your food is better than anything SHE could produce even on her best day” interjected Isabella, idly massaging her own bloated belly alongside her husband’s efforts.

“You think?” asked Gina, trying not to sound too shaken.

“Lover boy here didn’t marry me because your food made me sick sis” matter of factly declared Isabella with a proud and knowing jiggle of her belly.

“Maybe you’re right” thought Gina as she sipped at her drink.
‘Hmmm, more wine needed’

*****

“All I’m saying is” slurred Isabella as she wiped a chocolate smear from her lips with the back of her hand “is Gloria’s sister as fat as me?”

Gina looked her sister up and down before relenting a tipsy nod from behind her glass.
“I don’t even know if she has a sister”

“Well then she definitely isn’t fatter than me” asserted Isabella “and all good chefs need a fat sister”

For all the sayings about chefs that one didn’t ring a bell with Gina but who was she to argue with her most loyal customer.

“Isn’t it about time you ladies got some sleep?” suggested Benito with a cup of coffee in hand having opted out of continuing to drink some time back whilst the girls carried on.

“I guess” sighed Isabella “help me up!”

Benito sighed with a smirk before setting his coffee down and making his way round to his wife, taking her hands in his.

“Come on babe, pull!” groaned Isabella as she laboriously rocked in her seat.
Benito didn’t respond, instead giving a more forceful pull which did the trick of bringing Isabella to her feet causing her whole body to wobble.

Every inch a Bianchi woman, Isabella’s hips spread wide on her figure even whilst stood with fat thighs wedged together in her tight jeans whilst a smooth roll of belly fat hung over the waistband of said jeans, holding them up in their unbuttoned and unbelted state.

“Hey ten bucks say you can’t button them” teased Gina, resting herself on all fours over the arm of the sofa as she looked over her sister’s hulking midriff.

“You’re on” accepted Isabella as she reached under her belly to grab the two flaps of material trapped there before pulling them up over said gut ahead of trying to pull them together.
The Herculean task proving to be just that as the rolls of flesh squeezed together and against themselves in great bulges, a stretching belly button mocking the open air as denim refused to meet denim.

Finally though...
“Urgh fine, you win” relented the older and larger Bianchi sister as she let go off the flaps, allowing her belly to sag back to its original place against her bulbous thighs.

Gina laughed in her victory as she jumped up from her seat and strutted her skinny self past her sister towards the guest room where she stayed whenever she cooked for her sister.

“One day this’ll be you Gina” Isabella three after her sister in a mock sulk that was only appreciated by herself.

“Oh I’m sure it will” mused Gina with a toothbrush sticking out of her mouth and a coy tipsy smile on her face.

“You just wait then me AND mama will both laugh” teased Isabella as she waddled past the open door to Gina’s room.

“You keep telling yourself that sis” purred Gina.

“Oh I will” replied Isabella with a smile “now if only we had a third sister that cooked even better than you and got you to taste test everything”

“Tell you what Iz” announced Gina, lounging against the doorframe of her room, her shirt rising over her flat stomach as she spoke “you learn to cook and I’ll taste test EVERYTHING”

“Deal” cheered Isabella as she closed her bedroom door with muffled sweet nothings been fired her way.
Collapsing back into the large guest bed Gina stared up at the ceiling with her fingers interlinked over her stomach.
“You know, I think I can kick her ass now” she quietly pondered as she mentally ran over her business plans.

‘Yep, Gloria won’t know what hit her’ Gina thought as she curled up to go to sleep.
131142.title: Competency becomes complacency

choices: Nothing says I'm sorry like a gift basket laced with ulterior motive.
Buxom office bunny Danni is going to be the new spokeswoman for Gloria's biz.
Stage some incidents to trash Gina's public image.
There's deep rooted secrets in the Bianchi family they wouldn't want getting out
1311421.content:
Meanwhile, compared to the lovely night shared by the siblings (-in-law) in the Bianchi / Molina family, across the city of Trussel Gina's newfound rival was spending the night alone. Burning the midnight oil with a mix of seething hatred and dark ambition, Gloria kept the lights on in the bedroom of her penthouse suite as she looked through social media feeds on her computer. Her only companion a rapidly depleting chocolate-mocha mousse by her nightstand.

"How is she this successful?" Gloria said sourly, every picture of Gina the Italian bombshell had posted online making the side-lined entrepreneur loathe her even more. Not that Gloria had even been this skinny in her life but she would have killed, at that moment, for as sexy of curves as the young Bianchi chef. Or hell, if Gloria's size could be seen as sexy. She sort of missed the movement of body-positivity sex appeal that graced some of her other culinary big named (& big-framed) peers. Truthfully, it was less Gloria's physique but rather her acidic personality that kept her from getting the most out of her looks.

Yet photo after photo of Gina Bianchi, getting a feel for her life, was like another insult from this fresh faced upstart that had laid waste to her plans. Photos from the relaunch of the family restaurant when Gina took the reigns. Dockside photos from the fish market and the family yacht. That time she was dating a Hollywood producer. Canid snapshots from Gina's interviews on all the big name culinary magazines. And most recently, photos with her new partners the Heurta family for the revamping of Casa Su Vicindario and the groundbreaking of her next big restaurant.

Be it her pinstripe suits, fashionable looks or grunge wear for physical labor, Gina Bianchi was the 'femme' of a femme fatale natural beauty knockout, with her sun-kissed olive skin and sleek curves. Hell, her hair looked great even when she was chipping in painting the Mexican restaurant she swiped from under Gloria's nose.

"And all of her flake fans are just in love with her locks," Gloria commented on the comments surrounding these photos, too cynical to believe Gina's adoring fans loved her for her personality beyond her sultry looks. Her spoon scraping the bottom of her mousse, the scheming Kitchen Boss tried to think up a way to get back at Gina. The Bianchi family had been a big name in local business, of course, yet not one Gloria had considered a threat to her market. Up until Gina had showed up on the scene with the audacity to go toe-to-toe with Gloria and win!

"I have to destroy her," Gloria grumbled, taking a break away from the madness of hate-following her rival on social media to finish getting ready for bed. Clade in silk pajamas in gaudy tiger print, the hefty Gloria pieced together half-baked revenge schemes as she went about her nightly constitutional, the sting of her mouthwash matching her ill-will towards Gina.

Those who knew Gloria best, such as her sister, coworkers and friends, would have advised her to take a step back. To not obsess over Gina and stick to her guns. Normally, that was the trick to keep Gloria from setting her sights on eliminating a rival to the bane of actually running her enterprises. To be crude, Gloria having a dry spell in her sex life probably didn't help matters either. A good fuck would probably do wonders for her mood, outlook & expertise. It would certainly take her mind off of things, rather then dropping back down the social media rabbit hole as she settled back into bed with her laptop.

"How to ruin her imagine?" Gloria repeated, her oft repeated mantra this night as she shot daggers at the imagines of her rival. Going back to the media blitz about the change of reigns for the Bianchi family business, Gloria keyed in on the older sister, Izabella.

You can't hide a whale like that from the camera, especially one with almost a hundred pounds on Gloria. Obviously, most of the attention was on the bombshell Bianchi babe but her sister was hard to miss. With a large stomach and gigantic ass, in another life Izabella would have been the patron customer of any of Gloria's eateries, given how much she obviously loved her sister's food.

Reminded of the Bianchi woman phenotype, Gloria shifted attention to background shots of the two sisters' parents. Sofia Bianchi was no slouch in the backside department either. A short trawl through publicly available genealogy records showcased that all of the Bianchi women, back to the original immigrants to America and another branch in the home country, sported the hereditary genes for weighty buttocks.

Cut back to the present, another look at one of Gina's pics in her power-grabbing, sexily cut pinstripe suit. Not large enough up top to truly be considered an hourglass, although her budding Bianchi rump was tastefully full yet slim compared to the zaftig size of her mother, sister and cousins.

"That's a metabolic crash waiting to happen," Gloria mused, easing off into fitful sleep with dreams about how to knock Gina out of the picture.


"Good morning, Danni," Gloria greeted the next morning to her personal secretary as she reached her office.

"G-Good Morning, Ms Vandergast," Danni said, somewhat taken aback. Since the buxom blonde had come under Gloria's employ, true pleasantries were not present in her boss's typical mood. Not without some nefarious motive or power-grab behind them. As the last few days had be rife with Gloria's outbursts at being shown up by Gina Bianchi and the failed attempts to badmouth her business, Danni was prepared to buckle down for the inevitable Hurricane Gloria.

Quite unlike the meteorological catastrophe Danni used as a metaphor for her boss's mood, Ms. Vandergast hummed a little ditty to herself, thanked Danni for her morning cappuccino & pastry sampler, and even took time to read the comics page of the newspaper.

"Are you sure you're alright, Ms. Vandergast?" Danni asked some time later, with the unknown about what Gloria could possibly be planning weighing heavily on her mind from head to padded rump before she just had to ask.

"Never better, Danni." Gloria smiled. "Just savoring the anticipation."

"Anticipation?" Her bombshell secretary asked.

If only Gina was as easy to manipulate as Danni, Gloria mused as she glanced to her curvaceous yet skittish administrative assistant. Danni had a head for numbers and business, the backbone to some of Gloria's loftier ambitions, and without being too biased, more pinup worthy than Gina given Danni had a rack to go with her rear.

"Miss Bianchi has everything I would have wanted five years ago," Gloria explained. "Minus the old family money, niche cuisine and the boat."

"I, don't quite follow." Danni said, adjusting her glasses.

"The biggest thing Gina has going for her," Gloria continued, "minus the family connections, is all this great publicity she gets her direction. She's like some sort of movie starlet, makeup never out of place and a radiant glow whether at the spa or construction site, on top of her skills as a chef."

"She is quite the natural beauty, yes," Danni replied, thoroughly confused by this line of logic.

"A social media princess clamored by legions of fans," Gloria surmised. "And we're going to shake that confidence from her by sullying her image to all those followers."

Of all the inane plans she'd heard Gloria come up with, this one absolutely bamboozled Danni. "How exactly do you go about showing her up in front of all her fans?"

Gloria merely grinned, laying out the stages of her plan whipped up from last night's fever dream. (Mayhaps the cream in her midnight mousse had been sour?)
1311421.title: Subscribed to Destroy

choices: Despite the mockery, Danni keeps getting put forward and starts to enjoy it
Gina’s tiredness starts to catch up with her, not to mention the stress
Gloria enjoys hers and Danni’s success a little too much
13114212.content:
“Are you nearly ready Danni?” called Gloria, legs crossed at the knee with her hands delicately rested on said knee.

“Erm...nearly?” questioned Danni, sounding under stress as she called back through the dressing room door.

“No pressure, I can’t WAIT to see the results” grinned Gloria.

When all was said and done, Danni had been less than impressed with Gloria’s latest scheme but the pleasant atmosphere radiating from her boss was such a surprise the oppressed office bunny was willing to go along with anything.
Although, the blonde graduate did feel rather uncomfortable at having her usually angry superior picking out rather...flirtatious outfits for her.

“How’s it coming along in there?” asked Gloria again, feeling a little impatient now.

“Ok, I’m ready” sighed Danni as the door lock flicked open and she awkwardly stepped out.

‘Well well well’ thought Gloria as she eyed her assistant up and down with a wry smile.

Danni stood in the doorway squeezed tightly into a red dress that pushed her ample bosom up and clung so tightly to her office rump the taut material looked fit to tear.

“Well aren’t you a beautiful woman when you put your mind to it” cooed Gloria as she stood from her perch.

“Thanks Miss Vanderg...”

“Please, call me Gloria today” chuckled the Kitchen Boss “we’re not in the office after all”

“I suppose” muttered Danni as she tugged on the hem of the dress in a vein attempt to cover even an extra inch of slightly thickened thigh.

“Oh don’t worry about that, you look lovely” reassured Gloria.

“I don’t know, it’s not really...me” sighed Danni, placing her hands across her stomach which also looked a little too prominent given her office snacking.

“Sure it is! Let’s go get make overs and then you'll see it” offered Gloria, grabbing Danni’s hand and dragging her through the store, the blonde tottering behind on heels she was not used to.

“Vandergast, you know my account and send the rest of the outfits to my office” shot Gloria as they passed the clerk.

*****

“So Miss Vandergast, what would you say is your driving force today?” asked one reporter, thrusting a dictaphone at Gloria in preparation for an answer.

“Well business is a vicious animal and just that thrill of wrestling with it every day is the zip that I need” replied Gloria with a sickly grin and hands on hips.

“And what about the competition?” asked another reporter, the pointedness of the question instantly riling Gloria up internally, even if she managed to -mostly- remain composed externally.

“Sorry, could you repeat that?” asked Gloria, taking a moment to swallow her rage having perfectly understood the question.

“I mean people like Gina Bianchi, she’s been taking the city by storm right?”

“Oh for sure, Gina is great at what she does and...”

“Well hello everybody” came a sweet voice from behind the gathered press conference.

A soft mumble asking who the individual was washed through the gathering whilst Gloria smiled to herself.

“Oh sorry I’m late Miss Vandergast” giggled Danni as she weaved her way through the crowd with all eyes on her.

“Don’t worry Danni, get up here though so we can get some photos together”
Danni trotted up to the front and slid up next to Gloria with all jaws firmly on the floor as the blonde secretary smiled for the camera.

Whilst her dress up session had consisted of dresses and more formal wear, once Gloria had keyed into Danni’s clothes size it was just a case of flicking through the catalogues and matching with anything Little Miss Bianchi would wear.

On this particular occasion, Danni was dressed almost casually with a snug crop top that really made her chest pop with a valley of cleavage and some high waisted jeans that accented her bulging rear assets.
The outfit was topped off with some open toed strappy sandals, a long pony tail trailing between her shoulder blades and of course her signature red framed glasses with matching painted nails throughout.
Danni could have done without the exposed midriff Gloria had insisted was essential but it seemed nobody but her cared about that particular aspect.

“Now as some of you know I wouldn’t be where I am today without my amazing assistant and, of course, I am eternally grateful for everything that Danni does” beamed Gloria, an arm snaked around her assistant’s shapely hips.

“Oh Miss Vandergast, I’m just pleased to work with an amazing chef and all the...perks that come with that” cooed Danni, throwing a side glance over her glasses with a hand holding a flicked hip.

Since their outing to buy Danni some more appropriate outfits, Gloria had spent time teaching Danni how to be the promiscuous little honey she needed her to be.
And the media was eating it all up.

“Speaking of which how about we open up the buffet for our guests Danni?” asked Gloria, steering the situation back to where it needed to go.

“Oh absolutely” smiled Danni, strutting towards a covered table “don’t forget, leave feedback about your favourites”
In one swift motion Danni removed the cloth to reveal an extensive spread of Gloria’s most delicious canapés and finger foods.

As the gaggle of reporters descended on the table, Danni drifted away back to Gloria’s side and watched over the spectacle.
“Do you think this was a success Miss?” asked Danni

“I think YOU were a success” smirked Gloria, folding her arms.

“Hey Danni, over here!” cried out one reporter.

“What’s you’re favourite meal Danni?” called out another.

“Fancy sharing a bite Danni?”

“Well, sounds like you have an audience Danni” ushered Gloria as she stood back and watched the blonde girl get absorbed into the crowd.

*****

Gina was rushing back and forth from her kitchen feeding the last of the evening rush, something which had grown considerably since her brush with Vincent Scrowler.
With one last swing of the doors and a deep breath Gina wiped her hands on her apron before taking a seat.
“Another good night” whispered Gina, looking over her empire.

In the silence of the kitchen Gina could hear a faint buzzing coming from her locker.
“Wonder who that could be” muttered Gina as she climbed out of her seat and went to collect her phone.
By the time she got there it had rung off but a lone buzz indicated she had just received a message from, of course, Izzy.

‘You’ve got to look at this sis’ read the message with an accompanying link.
Gina immediately opened the link and she couldn’t quite comprehend what she was looking at.

“What is this Iz?”

“You can’t tell?” laughed Izabella

“It’s been a long shift Iz, short answers please” grouches Gina a little sharply.

“She’s got her assistant to be all ‘social media popular’” snorted the older Bianchi.

“What?” Gina really was struggling to keep up and trudged back to her seat, brow furrowed with fingers massaging temples.

“Ok, simple Gina needs simple words” mocked Izzy “Vandergast has got her poor bullied assistant playing the popularity contest, look at the pictures again”

Gina opened the article again and looked through the pictures.
Sure, Gloria’s assistant was pretty but she was no Gina when it come to the dressing department or the Instagram poses for that matter.
Awkward pose after awkward pose with the camera filled with cleavage or uncomfortable attempts to ooze sexuality with various food items in her mouth, the impersonation suddenly became clear to Gina.

“She really thinks that’s the way to win this now?” laughed Gina with an air of tiredness.

“Well, apparently so” snorted Izabella again “and on that note you are clearly tired and need to get some sleep so I will speak to you tomorrow”

“Yeah, night Iz” mused Gina, A’s she began to tidy up for the night ahead of leaving.
13114212.title: Hop to it bunny

choices: Gina tries to counter her weight gain with dieting, but the damage is done.
Izabella's influence means Gina really starts going to seed.
Gloria quickly capitalizes on Gina's plumping figure with more bad press.
It turns out Gina's not the only one putting on the pounds in either company.
131142122.content:
"Thanks for coming by!" Gina waved to the last of her late night regulars, then collapsed into a booth exhausted.

Okay, maybe she had bitten off more than she could chew in regards to expanding the Bianchi family enterprises. Just a tad.

"You okay, boss?" Said one of her new hires, a busboy.

"I'm fine, Ramone. If you could get me some water I'll come back to help you close shop."

Ramone shrugged as he turned. Gina supported her heavy feeling head in her hand. How did it take this much effort to stay on top of her budding rivalry with Gloria Vandergast?

Gina Bianchi was too headstrong and independent of a girl to appreciate the lessons of shrewd delegation. Yes, Gloria had more properties but she expended less total effort managing those than Gina did managing the flagship Bianchi locations, plus her assistance to the Heurta family to revamp their business as their new partner.

Gina really wanted to show what she could do beyond her natural cooking talent. She'd asked too many favors of her parents already for her liking; Marlon & Sofia Bianchi deserved to go on that cruise now that they had retired. There was some good crew on staff, of course, yet Gina was one who wanted to be involved with all levels of work at the growing Italian-fusion enterprise.

Getting good buzz out of the family announcement and Gloria's attempt at prompting a bad-mouthed review meant there was more customers than ever. And for as socially graceful of a girl as Gina, she tried her best to get to know her customers like extended family. She was also helping with the Casa Su Vecindario remodeling work on what were meant to be her days off. For someone as photogenic as Gina, staying on top of her social media spin to counter the blunt attempts of Gloria banking on the sex appeal of her secretary, Danni, required a lot more effort to achieve 'effortless beauty' in a vicious cycle of oxymorons.

Plus, Gina was needing to keep her phone on full vibrate and volume just to hear it during all the duties requiring her attention, as the buzz jolted her upright from her drowsiness.

"Hey, sis?" Gina answered.

"Are you still at the restaurant?" Izzie asked on the other end, recognizing the ambiance of the main family eatery. "I've been trying to get you all night. I thought you had brushed off me and Benito."

"Shoot," Gina cringed. "I promised to reschedule family night. Sorry, sis, it's just been really busy over here."

"I'll have to order catering from you just to see my sister at this point!" Izabella teased between munches. "Benito has been my saving grace with a impromptu order of fried chicken. Tell me you've had something to eat at the very least."

"Yes," Gina lied, betrayed by a stomach gurgle.

"I mean it, sis, you've been running yourself ragged lately," Izzie said with a caring tone. "This Vandergast bitch may talk a big game but I'm still in your corner. Everyone knows you're bound to beat her, so don't beat yourself up trying to outsmart her."

"Thanks, I guess. Gotta go, Iz." Her sisters words fell on partially deaf ears. Gina knew she should take some time out for herself, yet she felt just as guilty on leaving work for others. She was mired in her passions to make the restaurant the best it could be, to show off the Gloria Vandergasts of the world, that she went against the tired ache in her body to help bus up the remaining tables. Ramone looked like a good kid, albeit his work ethic could be improved. Gina figured the best way to increase his diligence would be to lead by example.

"Hey Ramone, what do you have there?" Gina asked some time later, swayed by a tantalizing scent.

"Your sous chef said we have to stale this out," Ramone said of some leftovers. Half a batch of leftover pasta, some terrimissu and some cannoli.

"Hold off dumping it all just yet," Gina said, stomach screaming for food. "Why don't you have some yourself?"

"Sure, I guess." Ramona shrugged. Now a week into a new work schedule, he was more keen to complete his tasks so that he could clock off to join some friends with online gaming.

So as Gina helped herself to some leftover portions, she ended up eating the most of those leftover portions.


"Senorita Bianchi, why don't you join us?" Oscar Huerta said.

"Oh don't mind me, Señor Heurta," Gina said, later that week, covered in paint and spackle.

"I insist," the patriarch of the refurbished family Mexican restaurant said. "You've been too kind with helping us out."

Indeed, the sweat off of Gina's brow had also wrought a knocked down wall, sheetrock, retiling of the main dining room and two of the replaced kitchen fixtures that had cooked this impromptu meal of simple burritos. Gina had hoped to commission more work from her brother-in-law Benito's company. However, the lack of availability, horrid quotes from competing contractors not wanting to help the 'enemy' and having picked up enough DIY skill for renovation from her sister's hubby meant Gina was practically volunteering her own labor at the Casa. On top of being the new co-owner.

Somehow, Oscar and Rita's grandkids, close to Gina in age, had managed to cook the burritos, refried beans, chips and some carnitas on only the flat top. Gabriel, the youngest, offered Gina a seat on the packaged materials being used for seats. He had a jovial smile and shaved his head, yet with Gina's involvement had newfound enthusiasm to turn the family restaurant around. "You're working far too hard, Gina. You need to learn to relax."

"This coming from my lazy, good for nothing bother who spent most of his time in at the track," said Guadalupe, the oldest. Hers was a plump, top heavy figure with a noticeable pair of breasts, with her own cooking talent on the rise from lessons from her grandmother Alejandra and Gina's input.

"I would make a big payout, sometimes," Gabriel said, with the tone of a downtrodden gambler.

"And now you're betting on a fine new Italian filly," Guadalupe snorted.

Gina had to stifle a laugh, much as her ribs ached, as the two siblings began squabbling in Spanish until their grandfather told them to knock it off. Although she had met them recently, Gina felt as close to Gabriel and Guadelupe as cousins. Lupe's talent was improving considerably from the burnt efforts from earlier, as Gina wolfed down her burrito.


It got to the point that Gina needed three shots of espresso to wake up in the morning. Such a caffeine boost was needed to keep up with her twitter followers and Instagram account, documenting improvements to her properties and keeping up her acclaim that Gloria and her busty secretary were threatening to steal.

"Mz. Gina Bianchi?" came a curt voice.

"Sorry?" Gina looked up from her phone, plastering a false, tired smile across her face. "Ah, Mr. Hinkley, from the planning review board."

"I'm just the clerk assigned to your proposal, Mz. Bianchi. You can call me Jason." Although offering a first name basis, the nebbish county commissioner clerk didn't appear all that friendly. "You've got the requested documents, I trust?"

"Yessir," Gina smiled.

"With the zoning reference, updated blueprints, and the copies of your financial records according to this loan?"

"Yes, and, uh," Gina realized she had hit a snag. Her big rebuttal to Gloria's earlier sabotage, in the form of breaking ground on a brand new big restaurant, had been an unexpected headache of paperwork with the city council. And now she realized that some of her paperwork was mixed up with materials pertaining to her partnership with the Heurta family.

"I'm just missing that form you requested," Gina said to the clerk's bored expression. "But I can have the scans emailed over. Why don't we, huh," Gina's saving grace was a bakery she had been frequenting across the street, "grab a doughnut and continue conducting our business?"


After three more days of long hours at the restaurant, further renovation efforts, and having to reschedule time with Izzie for the second time this week, Gina returned to her flat weary and beaten down. A bag of greasy fast food was her very late supper. There had been no time to cook for herself, nor extra leftovers to take for tonight. Keeping the lights dim because of her headache, Gina felt less than fabulous feeling pushed to the brink.


Gina had been something of a holdout given the 'curse' of the Bianchi family women genetics, both for backside and appetite. Her mother and aunts had always been large figures in her life. Izzie had porked out in high school, becoming elephantine with her marriage to Benito. Into her mid-twenties, Gina have remained a svelte yet voluptuous bombshell with her pert, rounded hips. Yet it turned out the cracks showing from her thin physique growing soft would be in the form of stressed-induced eating.

Which was why, some time later, when Gina just felt like she was getting a handle on managing multiple restaurants, while still keeping time for family, she hit a new snag getting dressed one morning.

Or rather, the feeling that her pants should not be this snug. She struggled with the button, then caught a look of herself in the mirror. The beginning of the end.

"Uh oh."
131142122.title: The Bianchi Burnout

choices: Gina puts on some sweatpants and goes pants shopping.
Gloria starts to get suspicious about Gina's absence from social media...
Meanwhile, Gloria's having some problems with Danni...
1311421221.content:
Fruit for breakfast. Just fruit. No skipping this meal, Gina. Healthy food. You need healthy food. Lunch, keep it light. They're gonna offer you stuff at the restaurants. Turn them down, or just have a little. Don't seem rude. Can't seem rude. But can't eat too much. Dinner... Can't skip out on Iz, but... eat only half of what her portions are. No, a quarter. Gym? Gym? No time. Gym would be optimal, but... no time. Count calories. That'll do it. Just count calories. Maybe run in place when unoccupied. No, that would be weird. Weird. Just count calories. Fruit for breakfast...

These were Gina's thoughts, 24/7. Every waking moment. She even dreamed of dieting. Ever since she looked in that mirror and caught that slight hint of pudge on her middle and saw the subtle traces of some new stress on her jeans' backside, she knew the family curse had finally arrived to take her. Somewhere deep down, she thought she was immune. Sure, every woman in her family was a fatass with an especially fat ass, but Gina was slim! Sexy! It couldn't possibly happen to her, right? Right?

That was what she told herself as she nervously spooned chopped up melons and pears and strawberries in her mouth. She was always hungry in the morning, but was she always this hungry? She genuinely couldn't remember. Just last year she would eat an apple every morning. Then she switched to two apples. Then three. Then she started mixing other fruits in. She hadn't noticed how big her breakfast food bowls had become because they never did much of anything to her waistline. Fruit was healthy, she thought as she spooned a glob of whipped cream into the bowl. Fruit was healthy.

Gina didn't have time to pack herself a lunch. She was an early riser, but most of that time was spent getting ready, showering, picking out an outfit (an activity that took her quite a long time), drinking coffee, and doing her hair and makeup. And eating her fruit. Can't skip breakfast. At night? No way. The moment she got home, she was out like a light. Unless she hadn't eaten dinner, then it was eat dinner and then pass out. That meant lunch would have to be eaten on the go or at one of the restaurants. The Bianchi family restaurant was often where she was at any point of the day and was, obviously, quite good, but it was soooo fattening. She could feel her hips getting wider just smelling the absolutely divine lasagna or looking at the sheen of the butter on the dinner rolls. Their salads... absolutely terrible. Gina sometimes considered getting better materials for the salads but... nobody ordered them anyway. It would be a waste of money that she could better allocate into their world class service and uniquely blended cuisine. So, no healthy options there.

Casa Su Vecindario was a little better on the calories. The food wasn't QUITE as good (no offense to the Huertas) but it was slightly better for you. Slightly. The problem there was the Huerta family was generous. Very generous. They were so grateful for Gina's help with their business, and they showed that gratefulness with cooking, on the house. And they made a lot. Even if she turned them down, they'd already be piling it on the table for her. Not wanting to seem rude, Gina would eat it all. If she tried to say she was stuffed, they'd see through it. They knew Gina had an appetite, an appetite that only seemed to get bigger the more she visited.

And then there was Isabella. Eating with her was like eating with the Huertas times 10. Izzy had been inviting Gina to dinner with much greater regularly as of recent, and she felt awful every time she had to reschedule or cancel. This meant that dinners at Isabella and Benito's were happening at least bi-weekly, sometimes tri-weekly. Izzy was a big girl with a big appetite, but they would prepare enough food for two of her every night. And every night, despite Gina's best efforts to say she was on a diet or to fast, all the food would be gone. Sometimes it was Gina's fault; Iz was an excellent cook and it was very, VERY hard to say no to her. But sometimes it was Isabella's. She'd playfully tease Gina if she couldn't finish, often mocking sadness if she said she was full or talking about how good the food was and how she was missing out on SUCH good baked ziti. And then they'd bring out the WINE. Oh Madonn', the wine. Gina's greatest weakness. And being drunk made Gina hungry. You could probably guess how that usually went. Every night she spent there, she'd end up stuffed to the gills and too drunk to drive, so she'd pass out on her back in the guest bedroom. And then, when she woke up, they made BREAKFAST. Enough said.

And so went Gina's thoughts. "Need to eat fruit. Healthy fruit," as she stuffed her face with whipped cream and strawberries. "Keep lunch light. Eat small portions," as she mindlessly snacked on ravioli in the restaurant's back room, absorbed in her bookkeeping. "Turn them down, just have a little," as Lupe gave her yet another burrito. "Only eat a quarter of what she eats," as she and Izzy shovel ravioli and garlic bread and wine into their mouths in perfect synchrony. "Need to hit the gym," as she rubs her round stomach and falls into a deep food coma, dreaming of running on a treadmill between satisfied snores.

One morning, Isabella, a late sleeper if there ever was one, was awoken early by her cell phone vibrating under her pillow. Benito had already gone to work, leaving his curvy wife to sleep off and digest the large dinner she had the night before. Sleepily, she answered, not registering the caller ID. "Mm, hello?"

"Sis, I... I need a favor. A big favor." It was Gina.

"Oh, hello!" Her tired voice perked up. She loved talking to her sister, even if it was a few hours before she normally got up. "What's this favor?"

"Do... do you have any of your old pairs of pants? From maybe high school or a little later?" Her voice sounded weak, resigned.

"No, sis, I'm sorry. I think I gave those to charity a long time ago. Why, what do you need them for?" She sat up in her bed.

"Oh, uh..." Gina trailed off. It sounded like she put her phone down for a moment. She picked it up again. "We just, uh, got a new hire at the restaurant and... she's got a big butt. She won't fit into any of the jeans we have for her. Yeah."

Izzy was confused. "She doesn't have her own jeans? I wasn't THAT big back then, remember?" She paused. "You sound off, sis, is everything alright?"

"I, uh, yeah, I'm fine. Just tired. I had a big night last night and didn't get much sleep. Speaking of sleep you sound tired so I'm gonna let you go bye bye love you!"

She hung up before Iz could get a word in. Hoping she was alright and thinking about her odd request, Isabella went back to sleep.

Of course, Gina was alright. A bit overworked, sure, but for the most part she was alright. What wasn't alright were her pants. She had outgrown them all. All of the ones that were presentable, anyway. Gina stood by the mirror and looked at her ass, a pair of black low rise panties hanging on for dear life, wedged into her crack. These panties had fit perfectly three weeks ago, but now they were stretched uncomfortably across plusher hips and a bubblier butt. She smacked some exposed butt fat and watched and felt it jiggle. Christ.

She turned to get a side view. Her butt, which previously had pushed out sexily in the perfect amount, now jutted out a few inches further. It wasn't THAT much truth be told, nowhere near her heifer of a sister, bless her heart. But it was noticable. And it was impossible to get any of her nice pants over.

Gina was so absorbed in her rump that she almost didn't notice another very obvious change to her body. She now had a little but bulging gut that pushed outwards pertly. Shit, a big butt she could spin positively. But this? She sucked it in and let it out a few times, giving it a pat or two on the bottom. "Shit," she muttered to herself, "I'm getting fat."
1311421221.title: Diet? Hardly gonna try it!

Danni approaches Gina off the record
1311421222.content:
As far as Gina was concerned, today was just a regular day; working hard at the family restaurant doing what she loved.
The awkward little pinches here and there of her clothes, although frustrating, were easily forgotten as she whirlwinded around the kitchen assembling orders and dashing them out to her ever loyal customers.

“Hey Ramone, nice work tonight” smiled Gina as she passed the young busboy, his arms laden with dirty crockery.
Ramone smiled back in return, concentrating on the load he was carrying and caught the kitchen door with an elbow before it closed on him.

‘He’s a good kid’ thought Gina as she weaved tables, offering well wishes and compliments to her patrons being careful not to catch any corners with her slightly wider hips.
As unwanted as her mild weight gain had been the young Bianchi had taken it in her overworked stride, so much so her avid social media fan base hadn’t even noticed.

“Here you go everybody” smiled Gina as she unloaded the meals she was carrying.

“Oh Gina, this looks amazing as always” cooed one of the waiting customers.

“Aww tha...ooph...thanks” replied Gina with a sharp grimace.

“You ok love?” asked a concerned member of the table she had just served, reaching to grab his plate as a delicate Italian wrist buckled under the crockery weight.

“Yeah I’m...fine” hesitated Gina, breathing deeply with her hands on her hips.

“You look a little pale dear, sit down for a moment” offered the first woman, clearing her space for their host.

“No no, I have to get number seventeens food out or...urgh...enjoy your meals...”
Her speech began to falter as she clumsily turned tail to return to the kitchen, the entire dining area spinning as she turned.
Seeing the sudden burst of light as the kitchen door swung open, Gina caught sight of a figure walking towards her as she staggered tripping over her own toes as the floor came rushing to meet her.

As darkness started to fall over her clouded mind the sound of Ramone’s muffled voice shouted ‘ambulance’ as a weight landed against her back where a table covering and the accompanying dressings had followed her down in her attempt to catch herself.

*****

For Gina, the world revolved around her for an eternity squeezed into mere seconds.
She remembered been loaded into the back of an ambulance, the clinical beeping of hospital equipment, the odd word or phrase; ‘exhaustion’, ‘lucky escape’, ‘relax’.
She could even remember the occasional face.
Her parents and sister had come to visit, some combination of the Huerta’s had come to offer their well wishes and even Vincent Scrowler had sent flowers and a card.

Now though, with sun peeping around a blackout blind, the young Italian belle was somewhere other than a hospital.
The soft furnishings she was buried in was one thing but the familiar photos on the wall indicated she was in Izabella’s spare bedroom.

As she blearily looked around, her head and shoulders aching deeply the door gently swung open as Izzie edged herself into the room.

“Hey you’re awake, how you doing sweetie?” asked her sister, a tray in hand and an ill fitting set of pyjamas maintaining her decency.

“I erm...I don’t know honestly” murmured Gina feeling like she hadn’t slept in days despite having just woken up.

“I’m not surprised, you gave us quite a scare there” replied Izzie, a look of concern furrowing her brow as she heavily sat on the corner of the bed, bouncing her svelte sister as the plush mattress squashed under her genetic bulk.

“What happened?” asked Gina weakly, collapsing back into the pillows behind her with her head throbbing.

“Well” started Izzie, awkwardly crossing one thick thigh over the other “the details are a little fuzzy because everybody was so freaked out but basically you collapsed at the restaurant”

“Oh god, the restaurant...”
Gina’s head began to whizz into overdrive panicking over the state of things in her absence.

“No hun, stop” insisted the senior sister as she all but pushed Gina deeper into the bed.

“But, Vandergast...”

“No Gina, this is the problem” sighed Izabella “the doctor said you HAVE to rest for a while”

“But...”

“No buts Gina, papa said you’ve got to stay with me for a while to get back on track”
Gina sighed reluctantly.
“He also said you would be worrying about everything so he told me to let you know everything is under control”
Gina found that hard to believe given how much she had going at the moment...

“Well papa has jumped back in at the restaurant for the time being and has taken in Gabriel to show him the ropes first hand and Lupe is making huge leaps at the Casa with some serious inspiration from you clearly”
On that note Izabella fished out her phone and pulled up an Instagram page with a very familiar face.

It did make Gina smile as she looked over the images with Guadalupe front and centre with just enough T on show to bring the allure with the restaurant vibes she was selling without harshing her grandparents traditional values.
‘She really has been listening to me’

“And Benito has been pushing your new venture forward and keeping the planning department on side with just enough sympathy to not look desperate”
Izzie gave out a dreamy sigh as she imagined her husband hard at work, a fidgety foot kicking the carpet as she chewed her lip.

“Thanks Iz...you’re the best...” breathed Gina tiredly.

“Now, you’re on my watch and it’s time for breakfast” insisted Izabella, pushing the tray she had brought in onto her sister’s lap.

“I dunno sis, I’m not really...”

“You’re in my house now missy” frowned Iz “besides, I recall a little deal we had where if I cooked you would eat it”

“Humph, I guess we do have that...” sighed Gina as she took up a fork and dug into the breakfast her sister had made for her.

*****

“How is it all going then babe?” pried Izzie between large bites of pasta and crisp fresh bread.

“Well Gina’s new development isn’t too far away at this point in the grand scheme of things” replied Benito between his own far more dainty bites “and the Huerta’s are just some finishing touches before their grand reopening”

“Wow” mused Izabella continuing to shovel food into her mouth “so they’ll be ready to go at the same time?”

“I’d maybe suggest get the Huerta’s open sooner rather than later, let them build up a little and then...”

Benito’s thought process was fine but he was interrupted by an unexpected sound.
Gina had burped rather loudly before scooping an extra heaping helping of pasta onto her plate.

“Well that’s something I’ve never heard before” smirked Iz as she flicked her fork around, watching her sister eating her third solid meal of the day.

“Sorry” pardoned Gina, placing her hand before her mouth as she swallowed “this is delicious and I haven’t eaten properly for a...”
Gina realised her mistake too late.

“We know” nodded Benito, pausing from his dinner as he spoke.

“Yeah, we are worried for a reason Gina” said Iz with that look of concern again.

“I’m sorry for making everyone worry” relented Gina, playing with her pasta in the process.

“It’s fine sis, just don’t forget to take care of yourself in the process” smiled Iz reassuringly, squeezing her sister’s hand.

“Well I guess I won’t have much of a choice with that one if I’m staying here” predicted Gina, delicately tucking into her pasta again.

“Right again” smirked the couple with Benito placing a hand on Izzie’s vast thigh.

*****

Gina couldn’t ask for a better host than her sister and brother in law.
Their attentive loving nature for each other radiated to the recovering workaholic as she spent her days recouping her energy and actually taking time for herself.

Although not her style, Gina had to admit that taking the time and observing her many projects from afar was pleasant in a way even if she did crave getting back into the fray.

First though, the Italian bombshell was going to get her jeans on.
Or so she thought...
Sure, the denim was snug as she remembered from before her accident but at least the buttons got close.
Now as she looked over herself with jean flaps grasped between her fingers she couldn’t even pull them together between the gentle bulge of stomach freshly stuffed from home cooking and two swelling cheeks out back fighting with all their plump might against her efforts.

“Oh...no...” panted Gina.

“Breakfast Gina!” called the senior sister from outside Gina’s room.

‘Jeans will have to wait’ thought Gina as she kicked the tight trousers off, stepping into a pair of discarded sweatpants that comfortably covered her wider hips.
‘Breakfast is important after all’
1311421222.title: Like sister, like sister

An enraged Gloria shows up with accusations.
