Each stop at her guilty pleasure, Sonics' drive-in, had her attempt to keep topped off on a liquid bloat through each leg of the road trip. Jafit had put on more than a university 15, becoming all the more voluptuous from an American diet.

They spent the last three hours ignoring the inevitable. Up until their respective connecting flights would tear them apart. There were no tears. There was little more than a hug to say goodbye. To Jafit it was bittersweet, she was going to miss a dear friend but return home. She had big hopes and wanted to get a job in a restaurant. Paul played it cool, yet slunked into her flight home knowing that the friend who had come to understand her the most was gone, on the other side of the world.

Sure they email and video chat. But it was decidedly not the same.

4 years ago, a snapshot of two different lives...

Pauline Spillum had loosened her preferences to be called 'Pauley' as the new preferred nickname. She was clever and street smart, yet as wayward as ever given her dark humor and continued rebellious spirit. She hated attempts from her sister, Rebecca, to reconnect. She feel into a like minded crowd of punks. She was a founder of a small scene grunge band that wanted to be experimental. She never had the music talent like her sister, the prodigy. The band partied a lot, although Pauley was often still the loaner of the group. She hit the alcohol hard, forgetting old limits. She grew a beer gut.

Jafit had gone from being a sous chef to almost running a restaurant in Madrid. Talent, charisma, and sex appeal did her wonders. Still, being handed the reigns didn't sit with her. She thought about her friend who had struggled to forge every part of her own personality to stand out, one she couldn't contact as often as she liked. Jafit wanted to revisit her roots, be her own standout. Show there was more to her than a head for stews and dangerous curves. She sought to start her own restaurant.

2 years ago, Tuscany, Italy...

Pauline was back to Paul. She had quite the band, they were lousy musicians, and it said something when you were the only one who was willing to stare at the realities of life without avoid them. Paul was no longer looking for that escape. She had mellowed out some, realizing a lot of things she saw in life as issues were of her own making, much as she thought she could always adapt. Much as she couldn't undo a lot of baggage with her sister she could look forward, and was talking to Rebecca again. She lost some of the weight yet had a stretchy belly that could balloon to volumetric size during her binges.

Plus, while she had been envious hearing of Jafit's success from afar, she jumped at the chance when Jafit invited her on a globe trotting vacation. Who else did that for a high school dormmate from way back when? Nobody but a rising star chef now with two restaurants in different countries who wanted to invite a dear friend to see them. And more then a childhood spent pin balling around the United States.

There was lots of wine. So, so much wine.

Present Day

"To top it all off, because Jafit forgot to properly vent the pressure cooker, it blew a hole in the wall," Paul said. "She's crying up a storm so after she shuts up I'm able to go out to the hardware store with only a translation dictionary and I'm sealing the hole at 2 am."

Cheryl, or rather Sheryleeanne from Choice Tidbits delivery, snorted into a spiced rum long island tea after having quite the unforgettable luncheon. Jafit's fantastic cooking plus a variety of cocktails from Paul, with only the ones following desert having booze in them, had left Cheryl feeling stuffed but giddy.

To say nothing of the impromptu hosts. Jafit, of course, was quite plus sized and it was easy to see where the weight settled along her chest and buttocks. She was't skinny around the middle, either, and a bulge was taking shape in her middle.

Paul, who quaffed quite a lot of fluid over the meal, sported a rounded potbelly under her clothes, having some sort of lycra material undershirt to keep her bloated midriff covered.

"Paul will help keep me grounded while I live in America," Jafit said, breasts resting on the edge of the table. "You should come to the opening in a few weeks. Toro Tapas will be my pride & joy. Maybe Paul will run it for me after I return home."

"Pft," Paul snarked. "As if. I'm not learning how to cook."

"Well, I don't know. I'll have to try," Cheryl said. It was getting awkward befriending Jafit and Paul knowing what her boss ultimate wanted to do with all this insider intel she would be providing. It brought Cheryl some pause.
14311.title: Over the Years

choices: There was a hidden microphone among the food. Gloria would hear all the secrets!
The food is designed to boost their appetites! Can't run a restaurant like that!
There was a bomb inside! Holy shit, Gloria!
143111.content:
"Hmm, I'll think about it." Cheryl stood up from her seat, patting her decent sized food baby for emphasis. She wobbled a little as she did, slightly tipsy. "The food was great, Jafit. And the drinks... also great." She giggled. "But I really should be going now. Thanks for the hospitality."

Jafit stood up as well, her rounded breasts swaying slightly as she did. "Of course, dear. Thank you for staying, as I know you're busy." She looked over to Paul, who was tapping on her phone. "I'm sure Paul thanks you too."

Paul grunted and continued tapping.

"Please don't take offense. Paul gets like this, especially lately. She has a... What do you say in English? A low social battery."

Cheryl smiled and laughed. "I'm the exact same way. Though the drinks helped!"

Jafit laughed in response. "I'll be letting her know you appreciated it." She strutted over, heels clacking against the tiled floor, and patted Cheryl lightly on the shoulder. "Come, I'll show you out."

They both walked back to the front door, which Jafit opened. "Feel free to come back any time. You were a wonderful guest and we'd love to have you again." She smiled warmly. "And do please consider my offer. It's not every day you get to go to a restaurant grand opening!"

"Thanks! But, how will I contact you?"

Jafit chuckled. "Just come over and knock. My doors are always opened."

"Oh, haha, okay! Bye then!"

Jafit waved as Cheryl walked away, rubbing her belly. "Goodbye! Hope to see you again soon!" What a nice girl, she thought as she closed the door.

---

"So, how was it? Drop off the package? You were gone for a while, did you get anything useful from that bitch?" Gloria hadn't left her office since she had formulated her plan and sent Cheryl off to do the dirty work. The blinds were drawn and she had candles lit. Weird.

Cheryl plopped down in her regular seat, still wearing the delivery uniform that had been procured for her. "Yes, Miss Vandergast. The package has been delivered." Her voice was a little down. The atmosphere here was not as welcoming as it had been at Jafit and Paul's.

"So, spill it. I see that belly. You had lunch there, didn't you? How was the food? What are they up to?" Gloria was chomping at the bit for any kind of info. She was stress eating chocolates, presumably to try and take her mind off of the horrible slight that Jafit had committed moving across the street from her.

Cheryl wasn't sure where to start. Gloria had been inundating her with questions, so she decided to simply go with the last one she had been asked. "Well, she's opening a restaurant here."

Gloria smashed a fist onto her desk. "Damn! That's the last thing I need. Fuckin' celebrity chef opening a place here. Think of all those people she'll be siphoning from me! All that business!" She groaned. "Oh well. We'll take that loser down, Cheryl. And that package you left, that's the first step."

"She was actually pretty nice, Gloria. I honestly think she might be interested in teaming up."

"HA!" Gloria threw her head back. "Team up? Me? Go in with ANYONE else? You're kidding. That's not happening."

Cheryl shifted in her seat. "Well, it was just a suggestion... Oh, she invited me to her restaurant opening, too."

"Oh?" Gloria leaned forward. "When is that?"

"She said in a few weeks. I don't know how many exactly."

"Shit, that's closer than I thought... Well, you're going. Whether you want to or not. We have some sabotage to perform, Cheryl."

Cheryl sighed. "Okay, Miss Vandergast." She actually really liked Jafit and Paul. She didn't know them THAT well, granted, but... probably more than Gloria. But they weren't paying her.

Meanwhile, at the Gamor/Spillum residence, the Vandergast package was about to be opened. What was the intended effect?
143111.title: Return to Base

choices: Follow Jafit, who discovers just how hungry that chocolate made her.
Follow Paul, who is about to have the time of her life.
Follow Gloria, whose stress eating is about to hit new heights.
1431112.content:
Hips swaying while she walked, Jafit made her way back to the Botanical Bounty Bouquet Cactus. The tall saguaro-like structure stared at her expectantly, like it was saying "come to me Jafit, eat of my fruit." At least, that's what it was saying in her head. She popped off another chocolate dipped fruit skewer and popped it into her mouth, slurping the goodies off of the wooden stick in one fluid motion. Jafit had had better, but this was pretty good.

She called to Paul, who was still on her phone in the kitchen. "Hey, have you had any of this fruit cactus yet?"

Paul called back, albeit in a lower volume. "I haven't."

"Well, you should! It's delightful!" Jafit sucked off another skewer. Even though she had just had a big lunch, typically large for her, she couldn't seem to get enough of this fruit.

---

Meanwhile, Gloria, still in her office, was placing a phone call to her favorite food scientist and painter, Chalchi Kayode, who picked up almost immediately. "Olá Vandergast! What's popping?"

Gloria tossed some jelly beans into her mouth, a favorite stress food. "I'm nervous, Chalchi. Are you sure this chocolate is going to have the intended effect?"

"First off, there is nothing to be nervous about, boss." Chalchi put down her paint roller, which she was currently using to slather an office wall in bright orange. "This chick, you don't even know anything about her? And you think she's got it out for you?"

"We have had this conversation before. I don't care if she has it out for me or not. Her mere PRESENCE is the problem. I told you this." She tossed in another handful of jelly beans.

Chalchi sighed. She really didn't get this woman. She should have known from all her time working with and for Gloria that there was absolutely no arguing or reasoning with her. "Fine. Don't you worry your pretty little head off, Gloria. That chocolate's gonna work perfectly."

---

Jafit was 35 fruit kabobs into the cactus, and showed no signs of slowing down. She downed them two at a time, shoving the long poles of fruit into her mouth and sucking them to be sure she got every single morsel off of the skewer. Next to her, Paul had finally arrived from the table, drawn not by Jafit's calls but by the unholy slurping noises she was hearing. "You're really gobbling those up, huh." Paul had only seen Jafit like this a couple times before. She thought of the time when they went to the buffet in Atlanta… Lord. She almost had to be wheeled out.

Jafit smacked her lips then licked them, getting off any excess chocolate. "I simply cannot help myself." She made a happy noise. "We will have to get that girl's company to bring us more of these." She popped another skewer off the cactus and once again slurped all the fruit off. "Mm, Do you, mmm, think- *GULP, swaaaallow* -that we can get them to make some edible arrangements for the grand opening? Maybe something shaped like a bull?"

"I don't see why not." Paul took off a skewer and nibbled off a chocolate covered pineapple. "Mmm, this IS pretty good."

---

"So, those are the basics of it. Does it make you feel better now that I've explained the way the chocolate works?"

Gloria had finished off her bag of jelly beans and had moved on to a box of Twinkies, unwrapping and munching on an oblong snack cake like a baby and its pacifier. "Sort of. I'm mostly concerned about how long it'll affect her."

Chalchi was sitting down on a metal folding chair. She had abandoned any semblance of work for right now, as Gloria being so flustered over what seemed like absolutely nothing to her was quite entertaining. "Well, the latent effects will last about 72 hours after it leaves her system-"

"That's not enough, Chalchi!" Gloria smacked her desk and took a huge bite of Twinkie, speaking with her mouth completely full. "Her restaurant opens in a couple weeks. I need them to be distracted by food that ENTIRE time." She spit crumbs as she angrily spoke, some sticking to her cell phone.

"Well," Chalchi didn't sweat it. She'd heard Gloria angrier than this. "You could keep her spiked somehow. Keep giving her chocolate gifts every few days or something."

Gloria crossed her arms and pouted for a moment, before unwrapping another Twinkie.

---

Paul and Jafit sat on the floor in the front room. Between the both of them, they had devoured all of the sweet chocolate covered fruit on the 4 foot tall cactus and all the fondant. All that was left was the inedible base. Jafit put a hand to her gut, which between her lunch and the cactus was pressing quite pregnantly against the inside of her sundress. It gurgled loudly like some kind of animal. She looked over to Paul, whose midriff was squishing against the button of her pants. Her eyes were shut tight. Jafit asked, "Do you need to go upstairs?"

Paul didn't say a word, keeping her eyes shut, but nodded her head yes. She touched her stomach lightly, her cheeks reddening.

Jafit exhaled. "We all have our needs. I understand. Do you have soda in your room?"

Paul nodded again, her face reddening even more. Even though Jafit was very much aware of her sexual proclivities (with such a loud fetish, how could she not be?), it never ceased to embarrass her whenever it came up.

Jafit's belly gurgled louder, thankfully changing the subject. "Dios mío… Why am I so hungry?" She stood up from the floor, brushing any dirt or dust that may have stuck off her ample ass. "I have a craving for fast food, so I'm going to get some. Would you like anything, Paul?" When Jafit turned around, Paul was gone. She shrugged her shoulders. More for her. She grabbed her car keys and went out the door. Her belly grumbled again in the driveway, and she scolded it and patted it. "Tranquila, tranquila. I'll have some more food for you soon, my little barrigita. Mierda, my thighs are going to be huge."
1431112.title: Feast

choices: It was bad news over there, and the owners were considering selling.
The place had already been bought out by a rival!
Danny's was closing for good. They weren't interested in being bought.
144.content:
"I... I don't understand." Cheryl looked up from her notebook at the despair in Gloria's eyes. "There are so many donut shops in this city. In fact, I think there's 3 in a 4 block radius from-"

"They aren't as good!" Gloria looked like she was about to pound out a sad melody on a pipe organ as she interrupted, a regular Phantom of the Opera, isolated in her office. "I've tried them ALL, Cheryl. All of them. In this business you need to know your competition."

"I didn't think we sold donuts."

"Well, we don't. But we sell desserts! And let me tell you, even I, Gloria Vandergast, would not even be able to come within a centimeter of how heavenly Danny's Donuts are. AND THEY'RE CLOSING!" She laid her head down on the desk.

Cheryl wasn't quite sure what to do. Social situations like this weren't her strong suit. She just sat there and said, "I'm sorry, Miss Vandergast. What... what are you going to do about it?"

Gloria rose up her head from her desk. An obvious, positive shift in her mood had occurred. "Yes... What AM I going to do about it?" She suddenly stood up from her desk chair, pushing it violently back, and pointed up to the ceiling. "I am going to do something about it! I will save Danny's Donuts! Or my name isn't Gloria Vandergast, kitchen boss supreme!" Then, she got back in her chair and rolled it back to where she was.

"Did that feel good?"

"You're damn right it did." She grinned real wide. "Cheryl, I need you to go to Danny's. Pick up some donuts. Lots of donuts. If this place goes under, I need my fill. Got it? Use the company card. And don't skimp out."

Cheryl got up from her seat, placing the notebook back into her pocket. "Understood, Miss Vandergast."

"Oh, and Cheryl? Tell me what you see. I need to know details. Why is this place closing? Who's running things? Just, anything that stands out. Got it?"

"Got it."

"Thank you, Cheryl." As she exited the room, Gloria's tummy grumbled. She was thinking about donuts.

---

About 30 minutes later, Cheryl returned to the office, carrying a huge stack of donut boxes. There must have been at least 8. Gloria wasn't counting. All she saw were donuts. As Cheryl walked in, her head almost invisible behind the stack of boxes, Gloria smacked her palm quickly on her desk and breathlessly said, "Right here! Right here!" Cheryl put them down where she requested and almost immediately her pudgy boss pulled the top box down, opened it up to find it full of jelly donuts, closed her eyes, and ravenously shoved it into her mouth, not even biting. She chewed heavily, eyes still closed, cheeks bulging like a squirrel's, and swallowed. Cheryl swore she could see a lump moving down her throat as she grabbed another donut and did the exact same thing. A ring of pinkish jelly had accumulated around her mouth by donut number 4, after which she dabbed the residue off and acknowledged Cheryl's existence. "I apologize. I'm hungry. Donuts are... my weakness." She picked up another and bit in, this time not swallowing it whole like a snake. "That's partially why I don't serve them here. I'd just eat them all." She chuckled with her mouth full, crumbs spilling out onto the top of her desk.

Cheryl, in the meantime, was not sure what to do. She mostly just sat there and watched her eat, politely smiling. Gloria must have noticed she was slightly uncomfortable, so she pulled down another box, this one filled with peanut butter filled donuts, and slid it over the desk to her. "Go on, have some. They're *BRUURP* delicious. Pardon me. And tell me what you saw!" She shoved yet another donut into her mouth, but Cheryl could tell she was listening.

According to Cheryl...
144.title: Donut Tinged Despair

choices: Despite the extra person, the group still is unable to finish the meal,
The group is able to finish, but they're completely stuffed!
Gloria notices her sister not eating, but instead feeding Ophelia...
Gloria ends up trying to finish the whole feast after everyone gets full...
A text interrupts the meal, looks like Gloria is needed today!
15.content:
As Gloria would look at her schedule, she would sigh a bit as she realized that she had no work to do. All of her restaurants were at peak efficiency thanks to hiring the best chefs she could, and as such nobody needed her to help in the kitchen. There was one major problem with this though, in that the kitchen boss did not like being being idle at all.

Pulling out her cell phone and a couple of numbers, she would call some of her close friends, namely her favorite co-chef Nicole as well as her own sister, Estelle, over to come and hang out. As soon as she finished talking on her phone, she would quickly set up the kitchen, and wait...

*knock* *knock*

"Hey Gloria, I'm here! Let me in!"

Quickly heading to the door, Gloria would open it to see Nicole, having arrived in first. Helping her fellow chef squeeze her meaty thighs and fatty hips through the door frame, the boss would hug her as she looked back to the kitchen.

"Ah, it's great that your here first, Nicole. You want to help me in cooking up for this hangout? I'll give you standard work pay..."

"Ha, keep it for now, not like I'm working! I'll help cook for free."

"Ah, glad that your still as good of a friend as after..." Walking up to the kitchen, Gloria would pull out a cookbook from a counter, before flipping through a bunch of pages, looking for a good meal to eat. After a minute of searching, she would pull out a few dollars from her pocket, to use as a makeshift bookmark as she would prepare to get ingredients for various meals to try out.

"So Nicole..." As Gloria would say this, she would pull out a footstool to reach up to the top of the huge industrial fridge she had, so she could grab some eggs. "How do you feel about the 'Henhouse Kitchen's Eggperor Challenge for Five'? I think it was three stacks of 5 pieces of french toast, five stacks of 8 pancakes, ten fried eggs, 30 strips of bacon, a gallon of orange juice, and a whole sausage and cheese skillet..."

"Ah, that sounds so amazing, my mouth's already watering..."

"Oh, I know..."

"You sure us two and your sister will be able to finish it all though? I mean, your sister doesn't exactly have the same appetite as us after all..." Nicole would help pull some sausages from the bottom of the fridge as Gloria would continue pulling ingredients out from the top.

"Don't worry about that... I'll save the remainder as leftovers and then us two can finish it tomorrow morning before we go out for work. Best way to save ingredients after all..."

"Ooh, great idea!"

As the two would finish to get ingredients, both Gloria and Nicole would start to cook the Eggperor Challenge for their meal. Due to the Henhouse Kitchen being one of the kitchen chains being owned by Gloria, she was quite talented in making the breakfast meals, but Nicole did manage to have a bit of trouble when it came to cooking eggs. Thankfully, since they were working together, Gloria was able to help with her partner's weaknesses, allowing the pair to do well as they continued to cook.

Upon finishing the very last of the challenge though, they would hear another knock on the door, and as the two would approach the door and open it, they would be rather surprised at what they would see.

"Oof... why does your sister live so high in this fancy hotel... and why did the elevators have to be closed..."

"Please shut up... Sorry for keeping you waiting so long sis. This is Ophelia, one of my... close friends. when she heard you were inviting me over she begged to come along, and I couldn't say no..."

"Oh, don't worry about bringing a friend with, sis..." Gloria would eye up and down Ophelia, noticing her obese figure and especially large breasts and belly as she did so. "Maybe I would have a problem if you brought a crowd of people, but if it's just one I don't mind... now let's eat!"
15.title: A Rare Day Off

choices: "She's just a fan of yours, Gloria. I'm sorry, let's not fight."
"This is what clinical obesity looks like, sis, I'm worried about your health."
"You don't recognize our long-lost foster sister?"
"We're, um...kind of...dating..."
153.content:
Before setting down to eat, the Vandergast sisters shared a brief hug. Although Estelle was the older sister and both had similar hair and skin, it was very hard to tell given their disparate weights. Estelle was quite thin, as Nicole had commented about to Gloria about how her appetite did't match that of the fat chefs. In contrast, Gloria was quite doughy, her profile almost hiding her sister's frame from view if it wasn't for Estelle being that much taller.

"How's the web business going?" Gloria asked. Estelle had a small but successful web design studio, which Gloria had taken advantage of when setting up the internet sites for her initial restaurants.

"Really great. I just landed a big commission from a yoga school. They've offered me a significant membership discount on classes, I could get you signed up, sis."

"Hard pass," Gloria said. "Keep talking, I was keeping the main course warm in the oven."

Estelle sighed deeply. "Yeah, of course." Watching her corpulent sister bend over the oven of her penthouse suite, she couldn't help but think about how Gloria seemed to be getting bigger as her sights were on achieving more acclaimed culinary accolades. Growing up together, her little sister had never been 'little' since they were kids. Gloria had been a butterball of puppy fat that continued into her overweight teenage years and her current burgeoning obesity on the sister's respective roads to business success. Having gotten a feel and eyeball for how plump Gloria was getting, Estelle expected it wouldn't be long before her sister would tip the scales a solid one hundred pounds heavier than her.

"Oh my god, sis!" Estelle noticed the quality of food Gloria brought to her dining table. "When you invited me to come over for breakfast, I didn't think you were whipping up a break-feast!"

"Hhmph!" Gloria snorted. "This was mainly for Nicole and myself before you brought along your own guest."

"Like I said, that was more last minute," Estelle explained.

"This looks so good!" said Ophelia, the stranger, who already found herself a seat at the decked out dining table as Nicole Harker put on the finishing touches of plating.

"Glad we've got somebody with a hearty appetite," she chuckled, before pulling herself two chairs. While Gloria fancied chic, oversized modern furnishings to meet the needs of her large lifestyle, Nicole's enormous hips required extra support. Oddly it looked like Ophelia, who was perhaps as large with her weight mainly focused on her stomach, had managed to just fit herself on one dinning room chair.

"Oh no," Estelle recognized the counts of eggs next to the french toast and pancakes artfully covered with powdered sugar and fruit chutney. "This is that 'Eggperors Challenge' thing of yours at the Henhouse, isn't it?"

"Did you expect any less of a breakfast from me?" Gloria replied. It could be said that the Henhouse, an all-day breakfast diner, had been her first successful chain of restaurants going on three locations.

"I thought it'd be a casual breakfast with my sister," Estelle said, being drowned out by the opposing bulks of Nicole and Ophelia tucking into their plates, unable to suppress their appetites any longer. "Without her latest co-conspirator," she added under her breath.

"Tch," Gloria took her silverware to her bacon and eggs. "This meal feeds five, sis, if you'd recall. Seeing as we have three of us who can appreciate a good meal, I think we'll easily round up to completing the meal after we consider your portion." She took larges bits of her rich eggs, crispy bacon, and fluffy pancakes. Gloria loved her sister, although over the past few years she'd been a thorn in her side about her weight ever since Estelle had gone on a health kick. Her 'big' sister kept the skinny genes in the family, a renaissance woman with an analytical mind suited to her web programming, matched with an artistic eye and sporty interests. Each of the Vandergast girls were very creative in different mediums.

All Gloria wanted was to give her sister the best tasting food whenever she played hostess. Estelle could do with putting some meat on her bones anyways, being too skinny in Gloria's opinion. She didn't expect her to go whole hog about it but Gloria wasn't going to sacrifice flavor what with Estelle considered healthy, cluttering her inbox with emails totting the benefits of egg whites and kale smoothies.

As for her friend...Ophelia was a surprise addition, although the type of gal who enjoyed her food as she dug into her five stack of pancakes and several sausages.

"So what is it you do, exactly, Ophelia?" Gloria asked, looking for a different topic of conversation.

Ophelia exhaled like a horse, finding room to breathe around the amount of food in her mouth. "Well, I'm in the programming scene myself. Kind of. More of a blogger really. I'd like to be a food blogger."

"Atta girl!" Nicole chimed in herself, after swallowing some fried egg. "We could always use more PR at the Henhouse Kitchen. And there's the big property Gloria's looking to open next month."

"Do you have many followers?" Gloria asked between chewing.

Ophelia took a bite from an outstretched fork. "*munch* Well, uh, only 50 or so I guess. But I love your food! I've got some of your cookbooks too!"

A big fan, then, although Gloria was looking for more of a 'big name' fan to bring extra acclaim to her restaurants. Conversation subsided a bit as the table continued eating. As expected, the two portly chefs and the mammoth Ophelia were putting away most of the food. Gloria brought up vacation plans she had with Estelle, as the two sisters had been on a cruise last year for the dual celebrations of Estelle landing a big client and the anniversary of Gloria hitting it big in the culinary world. This year's vacation plans were split between a tropical or alpine destination. Critiquing the cruise's food caused Gloria and Nicole to talk shop for a bit, discussing business.

Ophelia, of course, continued to enjoy the food. Although looking to be a big eater, Gloria noticed she was ruddy faced with beads of sweat forming on her brow. And then the boss of the kitchen noticed something she had missed during the previous conversation over breakfast.

Estelle had her fork directed towards Ophelia, with the obese woman taking bites of the offered sausage. The patterned continued with Estelle's last piece of french toast. There was only half a pancake on her sister's plate, making Gloria realize she had been feeding her food to this 'friend' of hers.

"What gives, sis!" Gloria was aghast. Generous wasn't a quality attributed to Gloria so she wasn't about to charge Estelle for a casual meal with family and friends. But it was an insult to her pride and sensibilities to have her own flesh and blood not eat her food.

Estelle, suddenly embarrassed at being caught feeding Ophelia, tried to play it off. "What? You made way too much anyways. You said the three of you would be able to round up to the 'feeds five' amount no matter what I ate."

"But I still expect you to eat!" Gloria exclaimed. "You come to my apartment and don't eat what I offer you?!"

"Geez, sis, cool down," Estelle said, backpedalling. "I had one piece of bacon and a pancake, I'm fine."

"You're aren't experiencing the full breadth of the meal!" Gloria roared. "It's called the Eggperor's Challenge for a reason."

"Ooo," Nicole let out a low whistle, eying Ophelia to see if she shared her sentiment. "Awk-ward, huh?" The oversized 'plus one' looked confused and unsure of the situation.

"Gosh sis, why do you always pull this crap?" Estelle retorted. "I didn't say your food was bad, just that you make too much of it! You don't have to go overboard on breakfast."

"You've been making cracks about my weight since my choice of swimsuit on that cruise!" Gloria fired back. "Yeah I'm fat, I noticed, I just wanted to treat my sister on our day off! No matter what I cook it's the same old story just because you can't appreciate it. And who's this strange woman you invited along anyways, huh?"

All eyes were on Ophelia, who tried to shrink into her bulk under the scrutiny. She looked to Estelle, concerned.

"No offense meant," Gloria said, although her tone was sour as locked eyes with her sister. "Seriously, who is she to you?"

There was a long, pregnant pause before Estelle spoke.
153.title: Cutting the Tension with an Egg Spoon

info.brief_description: Follow Gloria Vandergast on her path to restaurant tycoon in this weight gain interactive!
info.description:
Gloria Vandergast became her own boss. A self proclaimed Kitchen Boss, she aims to create a vast franchise of restaurants, resorts and other facilities that caters to the fat lifestyle. Fat people make the best customers at her eateries and she’ll do anything to keep them loyal.

Will Gloria establish the business enterprise of her dreams? Or will everything go to pot as her franchise crumbles beneath her? Contend with a peculiar customer base, manage eccentric employees, pounce on the latest the business market has to offer. And above all else, outperform Gloria’s rival Kitchen Bosses who want to sink their teeth into her business.




Unlike my other weight gain interactives, Kitchen Boss is less about a prefabricated story or lofty settings. The readers decide what happens next as Gloria becomes the biggest name in the food industry (however metaphorical, literal or ironically). With the starting concept we'll discover together just where the story goes in Kitchen Boss.

Beyond that a few minor guidelines:

- Feminine weight gain is the primary focus of this piece, although male gain is not forbidden.
- This story starts 'realistically' but more fantastic elements can be added at later chapters.
- Write your best, creativity is encouraged. Bring up new characters, venues and backstory.
- I only edit for grammar and chapter options, although keep the overall 13+ rating in mind as you craft your addition.

The cast and setting notes can be found here: "Kitchen Boss Recipe Book" 

Landmark Author Hall of Fame
Kitchen Boss sat dormant in my portfolio for a few years as I wanted to encourage the growth of the story by others in the weight gain interactive community, rather than any starting outline I would have envisioned. I wanted to give kudos to these authors for revitalizing my old story concept and shaping the initial storylines within!
